title: A Seahawks 53-man roster projection: Pre-training camp edition
description: Another crack at projecting the Seahawks roster before training camp starts.
fullText:
The Seattle Seahawks begin their 2026 training camp this Saturday, as the Super Bowl champions look to be the first NFC team to win back-to-back titles since Jimmy Johnson’s Dallas Cowboys in the 1990s. Yeah, it’s really been that long since there was an NFC dynasty.

There are plenty of intriguing roster battles on both sides of the ball, but especially on defense, where the bulk of the Seahawks’ offseason changes occurred. Before camp officially begins, I’m giving it another crack at projecting the initial 53-man roster.

Quarterback (3): Sam Darnold, Drew Lock, Jalen Milroe

Notes: Milroe might be competing not to get traded, otherwise it should be the same set-up as last year as long as Milroe doesn’t have a disastrous camp and/or preseason.

Running Back (3): Jadarian Price (R), Emanuel Wilson, George Holani

Cut: Jacardia Wright, Kenny McIntosh

Notes: Charbonnet is confirmed to be on PUP, while McIntosh is starting camp on PUP. I anticipate the latter being available to practice soon. Jadarian Price, Emanuel Wilson, and George Holani will surely be the top three backs, and it’s up to a numbers game to see if Wright or McIntosh earns a fourth spot while Charbonnet is gone. Most likely, they stick with three and shift one of Wright or Kenny to the practice squad.

Fullback (2): Robbie Ouzts, Brady Russell

Notes: Seattle is definitely going to be using full backs, and I hope under Brian Fleury that Ouzts can be something other than a tell that the Seahawks are running the ball. Ouzts had some tight end skills in college, so use it to your advantage! Russell is a “fullback/tight end” hybrid but really he’s there as a special teams beast.

Wide Receiver (5): Jaxon Smith-Njigba, Cooper Kupp, Rashid Shaheed, Tory Horton, Jake Bobo

Cut: Emmanuel Henderson Jr (R), Cody White, Ricky White III, Velus Jones Jr, Irv Charles, Rashad Rochelle, Montorie Foster

Notes: Either Seattle keeps five receivers or six, in which case I’m curious to see if Ricky White III can make good on his second season with the team. Seattle re-signing Shaheed and Bobo in the offseason essentially took away the drama as far as the wide receiver room.

Tight End (3): A.J. Barner, Elijah Arroyo, Eric Saubert

Cut: Lance Mason (R), Nick Kallerup

Notes: I have a feeling Kallerup is on the bubble, or at least competing with the rookie Lance Mason. Seattle keeping Kallerup on the active roster all of last season is encouraging but doesn’t mean his spot is now guaranteed for 2026. It may make sense to keep four TEs when the team runs so much 12 and 13 personnel, but I’d sooner believe the Seahawks would just make use of Ouzts or Russell when they want to go heavier and stash Kallerup and/or Mason on the practice squad.

Offensive Line (10): Charles Cross, Abe Lucas, Josh Jones, Mason Richman, Anthony Bradford, Olu Oluwatimi, Jalen Sundell, Grey Zabel, Bryce Cabeldue, Beau Stephens (R)

Cut: Federico Maranges, Bobby Hart, Amari Kight, Logan Brown, Christian Haynes

Notes: Brady Henderson has referenced and at least speculated Oluwatimi possibly being traded on an expiring contract. For now, I think he stays and Seattle will let him walk in free agency. Beau Stephens may eventually challenge or (in 2027) replace Bradford as right guard, but bare minimum I think it’s a wrap for Christian Haynes. I think Seattle may max out at keeping only nine linemen in the long run but the initial 53 could have 10.

Defensive Line (5): Byron Murphy II, Leonard Williams, Jarran Reed, Rylie Mills, Mike Morris

Cut: J.R. Singleton, Bubba Thomas, Brandon Pili, Uso Seumalo (R)

PUP/Injury stash: Deven Eastern (R)

Notes: Mills will be one to watch in training camp and preseason now that he’ll have a healthy offseason under his belt. Eastern missing much of OTAs and minicamp has me thinking he’ll just be given the NFL equivalent of a medical redshirt. Seattle lacks a traditional nose tackle—they pretty much have Jarran Reed as the nose—but Seattle may just stash Pili and/or Seumalo on the practice squad and elevate when needed. I’ve had Mike Morris on the bubble for seemingly ever and he’s still here, so I might as well back off until there’s evidence he could be let go.

Outside Linebacker (5): Derick Hall, Uchenna Nwosu, Dante Fowler Jr, DeMarcus Lawrence, Aidan Hubbard (R)

Cut: Jared Ivey, Connor O’Toole, Marvin Jones Jr (R), Jamie Sheriff, Jalan Gaines

Notes: I’ve not changed from the pre-OTAs projection. Hubbard is my lone UDFA to crack the roster. Ivey and O’Toole just miss out and at least one of the two gets put on the practice squad. The only reason I would counter myself to put Ivey over Hubbard is Ivey’s large frame as an edge could provide something of a unique look.

Inside Linebacker (4): Ernest Jones IV, Drake Thomas, Tyrice Knight, Chris Paul Jr

Cut: Chazz Surratt, Patrick O’Connell, Ja’Markis Weston

Notes: ‘Pooh’ Paul is still a great unknown in the Seahawks defense. This is just a pure hunch that could be quickly undone once we get to practices. Surratt was a cheap FA re-sign limited to special teams, but he might get the spot over Paul and others if it comes down to ST value. O’Connell is a perennial “ready squad” guy so I don’t worry about him.

Cornerback (5): Devon Witherspoon, Josh Jobe, Nehemiah Pritchett, Julian Neal (R), Noah Igbinoghene

Cut: Shemar Jean-Charles, Brandon Johnson, Michael Dansby (R), Tyrone Broden, Andre Fuller (R)

Notes: Witherspoon and Jobe are locks, Neal is pretty much a lock, so that leaves Pritchett, Fuller, Johnson, Igbinoghene, and Dansby as five guys competing for two spots. I’ve thought about it some more and for now, I think Pritchett and Igbinoghene have the inside track and that leaves Fuller and Dansby in particular on the outside looking in.

Safety (5): Julian Love, Ty Okada, Nick Emmanwori, Bud Clark (R), Rodney Thomas II

Cut: Maxen Hook, D’Anthony Bell, A. J. Finley

Notes: I think the final spot comes down to Thomas or Bell. Seattle has familiarity with Bell dating back to last season, but Thomas has more experience as a defensive starter while maintaining a high special teams count.

Notes: There’s nothing to discuss here. It’s like having three free spaces on the giant Seahawks bingo board.

There will be more regular roster projections once preseason begins, as by then we’ll have a clearer picture of who has the inside track and whose bubbles are about to burst. We’ll also have a metric ton of signings and waivers during that time frame that will make previous projections obsolete, so it keeps things fresh and exciting.

The only thing I’m certain of is that it will be very difficult to predict the bottom end of the roster, which is a testament to the talent level the Seahawks currently possess.

title: LeBron’s Free Agency Continues to Stall NBA Offseason
description: James’s ongoing free agency has lasted longer than his previous three combined.
fullText:
LeBron James continues to hold up the rest of the NBA while he decides on his next team.

It has been 23 days since James parted ways with the Lakers after eight seasons, leading to nearly a month full of speculation as to where the 41-year-old will play next.

NBA commissioner Adam Silver recently said at a CNBC event last week that the league can’t formally set its schedule without knowing where one of its biggest stars will be playing.

“We have to finish up the schedule, and where LeBron plays affects the schedule,” Silver said. “It will influence how we set the schedule—opening week, Christmas. So I need him to make a decision.”

But James is also holding up the rest of free agency around the league as several key players remain unsigned, with the teams interested in them remaining in a holding pattern until the NBA’s all-time leading scorer makes up his mind.

A common denominator among some of the main unsigned free agents: They are also represented by Jame’s agent in Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul.

In most years, the NBA goes into hibernation in late July. The league’s personnel and media descend on Las Vegas for Summer League when most of free agency is wrapped up and then head out on vacation until the regular season comes within view in September.

But James is extending the offseason and is holding up deals for multiple teams and potential teammates as he ponders his destination.

“We’re definitely working later than usual,” one front office executive told Front Office Sports.

James’s free agency has extended longer than any of his previous ones combined. In 2010, he went eight days before announcing he would sign with the Heat during ‘The Decision.’ The time between his departure from Miami and return to Cleveland four years later lasted 10 days. And it took just 18 hours for James to announce he was heading west to Los Angeles in 2018 .

All of James’s free agencies began with an ending in mind. He ended his first one on ESPN, second with a Sports Illustrated op-ed, and third through the Klutch Sports X account. But the length of his current free agency leads one to wonder whether it has the gameplan his previous ones had.

First in line for new deals after James decides are James Harden (Cavaliers) and Draymond Green (Warriors). Harden declined his $42.3 million player option for next season on June 29, the day before James parted ways with the Lakers, to sign a long-term deal in Cleveland. Harden, who turns 37 next month, wants an NBA championship and is reportedly willing to sign for less money to have James alongside him.

The Cavaliers could potentially offer James the full mid-level exception of $15 million if Harden takes a pay cut and if the team trades Jarrett Allen and Max Strus. But if James is fine taking the veteran minimum of roughly $4 million, it wouldn’t be necessary.

Then there’s the case of Green, who declined his $27 million player option with the Warriors in an attempt to help lure James to Golden State. The Warriors can offer James the full mid-level exception without performing any trades, but it would require Green to sign for less. Green is another Klutch client James is keeping in limbo.

Peyton Watson, another Klutch client, and Jalen Duren, round out some of the other major un-signed free agents all awaiting a decision from James.

The post LeBron’s Free Agency Continues to Stall NBA Offseason appeared first on Front Office Sports.

title: Mets reportedly make trade decision on $26 million All-Star starting pitcher
description: The Mets have a clear trade plan in mind.
fullText:
Mets reportedly make trade decision on $26 million All-Star starting pitcher originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

With the MLB trade deadline less than two weeks away, the New York Mets are one of the few clear sellers.

New York's season has been a disappointment, to say the least.

With one of the highest payrolls in the sport, the Mets have plummeted to the bottom of the National League standings.

While Francisco Lindor trade rumors have circled, the likelihood of a deal coming to fruition seems unlikely.

In terms of the starting rotation, the Mets have one obvious trade candidate: Freddy Peralta, an impending free agent. Extension talks between the two sides are virtually nonexistent.

The same can be said about two-time All-Star Clay Holmes, according to FanSided's Robert Murray.

"At this point, an extension between right-hander Clay Holmes and the New York Mets ahead of the Aug. 3 trade deadline appears unlikely, multiple major-league sources say," Murray wrote.

Holmes fractured his fibula in May and has been on the IL ever since. Before the injury, he posted a 2.39 ERA with a 55.3% ground ball rate.

That performance followed a great 2025 season in Queens, his first since moving from the bullpen to the rotation.

"Things can change with one phone call, especially at this time of the year, so nothing can be totally ruled out," Murray added. "But Holmes is expected to draw significant trade interest in the coming days as he’s getting closer to returning from a fractured fibula that’s sidelined him since mid-May."

MORE: Dodgers' Shohei Ohtani injury update essentially ends Cy Young chances

An extension with Holmes makes sense for the Mets if the two sides can reach an agreement.

Chances are, New York's front office already has its sights set on returning to contention in 2027.

At the very least, locking Holmes up long-term would help with the expected departure of Peralta.

title: NHL Prospect Pool Overview '26-27: New Jersey Devils Hope Their Prospect Pool Provides Depth Roster Players
description: The New Jersey Devils are stuck between having a mediocre prospect pool, but also not being playoff locks each season. With their current crop of prospects, they are hoping quite a few turn into depth pieces down the road.
fullText:
The New Jersey Devils prospect pool has fluctuated between very good and underwhelming over the last few years.

They’ve added some solid prospects and traded them away. They’ve also had some prospects age out or fall out of relevance as well. The Devils’ pipeline has some solid players, but they lack surefire bets beyond a couple of guys at the top of the pool.

With the NHL off-season, we have the perfect opportunity to look at each team’s prospect pool, going in alphabetical order, which means it’s New Jersey’s turn.

In our annual series, Tony Ferrari's digging into each team's top prospects, the strengths and weaknesses of its pipeline, an overview of its latest draft class and positional depth chart, and who could be next in line for an NHL chance.

Aside from some exceptions, a player who no longer holds NHL rookie eligibility is considered graduated and no longer a prospect for the purposes of these exercises.

The most impactful move for the Devils' prospect pool was their trade of Simon Nemec, the 2021 second overall pick. Nemec has long been rumored in trade talks, and the Devils finally pulled the trigger, acquiring conditional first-round picks in the 2027 NHL draft and 2028 NHL draft (both top ten protected), a second-round pick in the 2026 NHL draft, and defense prospect Etienne Morin. It gave Nemec a fresh start and the Devils additional draft capital to either add to their NHL roster via trade or bolster their prospect pool over the next couple of years.

The Devils' new GM, Sunny Mehta, inherited a prospect pool that is a bit underwhelming despite not being a consistent playoff contender. They have a couple of young players on their roster, with Luke Hughes being the most integral. The youngest Hughes brother will need a bounce-back year after his play seemed to dip last season. The Devils looked lost last year, and Hughes' game followed suit. The offensively gifted blueliner remains one of their most important building blocks moving forward.

Another young player that should see a good amount of NHL time this year is Lenni Hameenaho. He’s an intelligent player who picks apart opponents as a passer and seems to understand how to connect plays. He’s exceeded his rookie eligibility, but he’s looking to establish himself as an NHLer. His versatility will help keep him in the lineup.

Benjamin Kevan is a high-flying forward who has game-breaking speed. He is constantly looking to put defenders on their heels. His freshman season at Arizona State was merely fine, putting up 10 points in 34 games. He should get a bit more opportunity this year, and if he can gain some confidence attacking the net like he was in his draft year, he should be a really effective NCAA scorer and get his development back on track.

Another player looking to bounce back in his sophomore season is Conrad Fondrk. His passing game is diverse and incredibly skilled, showing several interesting passing habits. Fondrk isn’t afraid to shoot the puck either, getting to the middle and picking corners from excellent shooting positions. If he can get back on track this season, the Devils prospect pool looks a bit better because of it.

A very solid two-way forward who has great size and plays intelligently at both ends of the ice, Gustav Hillstrom is looking to showcase his offensive tools a bit more at the SHL level this upcoming season. Last year, he was a force at the J-20 level and a non-factor offensively at the SHL level. His passing is his best asset, but he didn’t seem to have the pace to keep up against men. This will be a big season for Hillstrom’s development.

The Nemec trade netted the Devils a blueline prospect in Morin, an interesting two-way puck mover who may not have quite the upside of Nemec, but he has shown flashes of solid play. Last year was his first pro season, spent mostly at the AHL level with a stint in the ECHL. Morin can bring some physicality in the defensive zone, but he needs to pick his spots a bit more consistently. Morin will play at the AHL level in his first season in the Devils’ system.

After a couple of solid seasons in the KHL, Anton Silayev is coming to the AHL this upcoming season. The 6-foot-7 defender is a specialist in his own zone, killing play with his length and physicality. Silayev has some nice passing tools and a heavy shot, but his offensive play isn’t likely to ever become a major part of his game.

Seamus Casey remains one of the most interesting players in New Jersey’s system. His puck-moving ability and intelligent transition play are impressive. He is a bit of an undersized defenseman, but he’s consistently found a way to make a positive impact. Casey has been stuck as a tweener to this point, not getting consistent NHL minutes while being near dominant at the AHL level. This season, he will look to solidify his spot in the NHL.

In his first full season at the NCAA level, Mikhail Yegorov had a solid year, but it was a step back from his dominant run in the second half of last season. The 6-foot-5 Russian is one of the most intriguing goalie prospects in the game, with stretches of truly dominant play matched by stretches that leave you feeling underwhelmed. He plays a calm game, not looking to get out of his crease too much and making small movements to ensure that he isn’t overcommitting.

Lenni Hameenaho (RW), Luke Hughes (D), Seamus Casey (D)

Round 1, 12th overall - Alexander Command, C, Orebro Jr (Swe.)

Round 2, 37th overall - Matias Vanhanen, LW, Everett (WHL)

Round 2, 44th overall - Nikita Shcherbakov, D, Ufa Jr (Rus.)

Round 4, 119th overall - Lavr Gashilov, C, Yekaterinburg Jr (Rus.)

Round 5, 149th overall - Daniil Rusakovich, G, Babruysk Jr (Rus.)

Round 6, 172nd overall - Luke Wilfley, C, Portland (WHL)

Round 7, 222nd overall - Quinn McKenzie, C, Sault Ste. Marie (OHL)

The New Jersey Devils had a new GM at the helm for the 2026 NHL draft, and it was certainly an interesting draft, to say the least. They took Alexander Command well ahead of most projections. He was a name many viewed as a potential riser on the draft floor because of the lack of center prospects, but it was shocking nonetheless.

Command is a high-motor center who plays with a relentless mindset. He’s always hunting the puck down, playing a strong two-way game, and he’s always done an excellent job of finding the next play to make when the puck is on his stick. He’s a play connector. Command has a very good shot, and he can flash some nifty passing at times as well. The newest Devil simply lacks that elite-level skill. Everything is very good across the board, but nothing is truly great outside of his motor. At the end of the day, 12th overall might be a bit high for what he turns out to be, but he’s going to be an important piece regardless.

Matias Vanhanen was just four days away from being a first-time eligible draft prospect this season. Still, the re-entry candidate made the most of his first season in the WHL, putting up 87 points in 62 games, showcasing the slick passing game that netted him 66 assists. Vanhanen is an excellent rush attacker, working well as a puck carrier or support player in transition. He needs to get stronger and develop his shot, but he has some really interesting upside. He’s heading to Boston College in the NCAA next season.

The Devils nabbed 6-foot-5 Russian defenseman Nikita Scherbakov. His floor is as safe as it gets outside of the top tier of players with incredible mobility and size. Scherbakov is a very good defensive play killer. He uses his length extremely well and has no issue using his body to close out along the wall. He’s a very good passer, particularly on the breakout. If he can become a bit more of a consistent offensive player, he could easily be an impact top-four defender that makes plays at both ends of the ice.

New Jersey took a swing on a high-end playmaker in Lavr Gashilov. He plays a high-pace game and loves to fire pucks all over the ice as a passer. He needs to work on multiple aspects of his game, especially off the puck. His defensive effort is mediocre when it’s at its best, and he isn’t the one who generally goes and gets the puck on the forecheck. Gashilov’s passing is undeniably high-end, but the rest of his game is a work in progress.

Daniil Rusakovich is an athletic netminder who moves well around his crease, but he’s a bit of a wild man, constantly forced to use his athleticism to make a save when better tracking and improved technical play could have made things easier on him. It’s a bet on a netminder with elite athletic tools from the NHL’s current goalie factory, Russia.

New Jersey took a defensive center in Luke Wilfley. He puts in the work in his own end, looking to annoy the life out of opposing forwards and turn pucks over. He can play a bit of a heavier game when needed as well. He must develop more on-puck skill, but he showed flashes of promising passing and a heavy shot, albeit quite inconsistently.

The final pick of New Jersey’s draft was Quinn McKenzie, an undersized forward who plays with pace and has a very good shot. He has a great motor and battles every time he is on the ice. He’s heading to Penn State after next season, and he should thrive in the college environment because of his commitment to working his tail off and an offensive game that thrives on getting the puck moving around the zone.

The Devils don’t have a particularly strong prospect pool if we are being blunt, but they have done a good job of adding left-shot defensemen over the last few years. Silayev could be a top-four option. Morin, acquired in the Nemec trade, is a crafty puck mover. Shcherbakov is a really interesting prospect that they added in the draft this year.

Even players like Topias Vilen and Daniil Orlov have some interesting traits that could make them solid depth pieces if used correctly at the pro level. It’s not that the left side is burgeoning with talent, but they have a few interesting prospects that could be useful at the bare minimum, which can’t be said about most positions in their pipeline.

Beyond Casey, the right side of their blueline pipeline is less than enviable. They have a couple of swings in the dark with Sigge Holmgren and Charlie Leddy, but neither looks like a true NHL projection. The Devils moving Nemec really depleted the right side because he was the future there. Now they have Casey and a hope and a dream.

Last season was Fondrk’s freshman year at Boston University, and it went about as poorly as it could have. Inconsistent, rusty play was sandwiched between rushing back from an injury from the end of the prior season and ending the year with another injury. Fondrk never really got his feet under him, and he admitted throughout the season that he was pushing himself to play despite not being even close to 100% from the broken leg that he suffered at the end of his draft year. His underwhelming play lasted just 25 games, as his freshman year came to an end because of another injury.

Despite that incredibly unfortunate series of events, Fondrk remains one of the most interesting players in the Devils’ system. He is a deceptive dual-threat scorer who can make passes under pressure, cut into a lane to get his shot off or find space to fire a one-timer at the net. His speed never really recovered last season, and it had been an asset up to that point, so seeing a return to form on that front should help him rebound in general next season.

This is cheating a bit, considering Hameenaho spent 33 games in the NHL in the latter half of last season, but he should be able to lock down a full-time gig in camp if nothing unexpected happens. Even if he is playing in the bottom-six, Hameenaho has the intelligence to do it. His facilitation ability and willingness to play his role as a cog in the machine are what will make him successful in the lower half of the lineup. Hameenaho could be exactly what the Devils need, as they’ve sorely been missing the depth scoring that can help take the load off their top-end talent.

LW: Lenni Hameenaho, Matias Vanhanen, Cole Brown, Josh Filmon, Samu Salminen, Shane Lachance

C: Alexander Command, Conrad Fondrk, Gustav Hillstrom, Lavr Gashilov, Mason Moe

RW: Benjamin Kevan, Cam Squires, David Rosival, Matyas Melovsky

LD: Anton Silayev, Etienne Morin, Nikita Shcherbakov, Daniil Orlov, Topias Vilen, Daniil Karpovich

RD: Seamus Casey, Sigge Holmgren, Charlie Leddy

G: Mikhail Yegorov, Trenton Bennett, Veeti Louhivaara, Tyler Brennan, Daniil Rusakovich, Jakub Malek

For a deeper dive into the prospect pool with player rankings, check out the Yearbook and Future Watch editions of The Hockey News in print.

title: Guardians' Gavin Williams achieves rare Immaculate Inning vs. Twins
description: They call him the Big Rig, and he was untouchable in the fourth inning on Thursday.
fullText:
Guardians' Gavin Williams achieves rare Immaculate Inning vs. Twins originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Gavin Williams has broken out in a massive way for the Cleveland Guardians in the last year-plus.

They call him the "Big Rig," and he's an underratedly dominant right-handed pitcher.

Never has that been more clear than Thursday's fourth inning against the Minnesota Twins.

That's when Williams took the mound and fired an Immaculate Inning.

Immaculate Innings, Cleveland history:

Today: Gavin Williams

Sept. 27, 2022: Enyel De Los Santos

Sept. 28, 2020: Zach Plesac

July 7, 2017: Carlos Carrasco

June 4, 2014: Justin Masterson

— Zack Meisel (@ZackMeisel) July 23, 2026

MORE: Yankees' Jose Caballero is pushing the limit of the rules

An Immaculate Inning is when a pitcher throws nine pitches to get three outs. They're all strikes, so it accounts for three strikeouts.

Strike one, strike two, strike three, one out.

Strike one, strike two, strike three, two outs.

Strike one, strike two, strike three, three outs.

Here's how it looked for Williams:

IMMACULATE INNING 🤯

Gavin Williams strikes out the side on nine pitches! pic.twitter.com/n93Uen7EIb

Maybe most impressive on this particular day: Williams did it against 2-3-4 in the Minnesota order, a strong trio of Byron Buxton, Ryan Jeffers and Josh Bell.

title: Washington football commit ranked among top 10 WRs in 2027 class
description: One of Washington's top commits in the 2027 class found himself among the nation's top-ranked wide receivers in the latest Rivals300 update.
fullText:
Even though a shoulder injury will keep him off the field for his senior season, four-star Washington Huskies wide receiver commit Braylon Pope is still considered among the nation's best prospects in the 2027 class.

In the latest update of the Rivals300, the 6-foot-3, 190-pound product of in-state Sumner High School remained among the nation's elite prospects, as the recruiting site ranked Pope as the No. 53 overall prospect and No. 6 wide receiver.

Pope was the first player to pledge himself to Washington's 2027 class after pulling in 52 receptions for 869 yards and 12 touchdowns during his junior season, while showcasing a skill set that fits perfectly on the outside of Jedd Fisch's offense.

Top WRs in the updated Rivals300 rankings⚡️https://t.co/TKcY5RGK2s pic.twitter.com/QAC6hvfu2K

— Rivals (@Rivals) July 15, 2026

"Smooth, coordinated pass-catcher with one of the more complete skill sets among outside wide receiver prospects in the 2027 cycle," Rivals' Charles Power wrote in his evaluation. "Has projectable size, measuring in at over 6-foot-2 and around 190 pounds prior to his junior season. A fluid athlete with natural movement patterns. One of the better athletes among bigger wide receiver prospects in the cycle. Projects as a plus route runner. Flashes a quality release package and can stack defensive backs off the line of scrimmage. Able to create separation with his change of direction and can take the top off on vertical routes with his long speed."

"A quality ball tracker and shows the ability to win in contested catch situations. A red zone target on jump balls and back shoulder throws for his high school team. Has good YAC ability and doubles as a dangerous return man, taking several kicks to the house as a junior. Finished his junior season with 55 catches for 869 yards and 12 touchdowns. Improved his track times as a junior, running an impressive 10.75 mark in the 100 meters. Does not play the strongest competition and can continue to get stronger at the catch point, along with adding to his route tree. Has a highly projectable skill set given the combination of size, speed, and coordination. Could end up as one of the top wide receivers in the cycle and as an early-round draft pick with continued development."

Pope is one of two receivers ranked inside the Rivals300 update alongside four-star Tre Moore, who is sitting at No. 218 overall and as the No. 33 player at his position.

This article originally appeared on Huskies Wire: Washington football commit ranked among top 10 WRs in 2027 class

title: 🗣️ Have your say: who deserves to win the Ballon d'Or?
description: It’s all very unclear.With the end of the 2026 World Cup, the curtain has come down on this football season.So now it’s time to take stock and look at THE big question that will keep us on edge ov...
fullText:
With the end of the 2026 World Cup, the curtain has come down on this football season.

So now it’s time to take stock and look at THE big question that will keep us on edge over the coming months: who should win the 2026 Ballon d’Or?

And for the first time in a very long while, no clear favorite seems to stand out. Michael Olise and Harry Kane have put up absolutely exceptional numbers but failed to win the two major trophies: the Champions League and the World Cup.

For his part, Kylian Mbappé also shone with extraordinary stats despite an injury-hit start to 2026. His exceptional World Cup speaks in his favor, but his trophyless season could end up costing him dearly.

Lamine Yamal, meanwhile, had a less dazzling season statistically, but he was named La Liga Player of the Season and, above all, won the World Cup. However, his slightly more subdued role during the tournament could work against him.

Parisians Ousmane Dembélé and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia could benefit from the vote being split because of the World Cup. Named the best player in the Champions League, the Georgian could make up for his absence from the World Cup, but he starts from a long way back.

Finally, Rodri and Messi are also natural contenders for football’s ultimate prize. The former won the World Cup and was named the tournament’s best player, while the latter, despite an exceptional run, fell at the final hurdle.

So we’re asking you: who should win the 2026 Ballon d’Or? To help you decide, we’ve put together a recap below of the favorites’ trophies and individual honors this season.

Harry Kane: Bundesliga winner, German Cup winner, German Super Cup winner, European Golden Shoe.

Khvicha Kvaratskhelia: Champions League winner, Ligue 1 winner, Trophée des Champions winner, Intercontinental Cup winner, UEFA Super Cup winner, Champions League Player of the Season.

Kylian Mbappé: 2026 World Cup Golden Boot, La Liga top scorer, Champions League top scorer.

Lamine Yamal: La Liga winner, Spanish Super Cup winner, 2026 World Cup winner, La Liga Player of the Season.

Lionel Messi: 2025 MLS Cup winner, 2025 MLS Player of the Season, 2025 MLS top scorer.

Michael Olise: Bundesliga winner, German Cup winner, German Super Cup winner, Bundesliga Player of the Season, Bundesliga top assist provider, Champions League top assist provider, World Cup top assist provider.

Ousmane Dembélé: Champions League winner, Ligue 1 winner, Trophée des Champions winner, Intercontinental Cup winner, UEFA Super Cup winner.

Rodri: 2026 World Cup winner, FA Cup winner, League Cup winner, 2026 World Cup Player of the Tournament.

title: Bowling: Pluhowsky and Via return to action with Team USA
description: Jul. 23—The top of the standings is always the goal for Shannon Pluhowsky, but the top of the podium is unforgettable when she is wearing the red, white and blue. "When you're wearing USA across your back it's very special because so few people get to do that," Pluhowsky said. "Even now when I hear the National Anthem, my mind will wander back to that moment on top of the podium when it ...
fullText:
Jul. 23—The top of the standings is always the goal for Shannon Pluhowsky, but the top of the podium is unforgettable when she is wearing the red, white and blue.

"When you're wearing USA across your back it's very special because so few people get to do that," Pluhowsky said. "Even now when I hear the National Anthem, my mind will wander back to that moment on top of the podium when it played."

The Dayton area bowler will get another chance to hear the anthem as she was recently selected by the United States Bowling Congress National Selection Committee to compete for Team USA at the 2026 International Bowling Federation World Cup, from Oct. 12-21 at Max Munich Bowling in Germany. It will be Pluhowsky's 26th year competing with Team USA.

"I was 18 when I first traveled internationally with the team," she said. "I was a freshman at Nebraska and basically moved into the dorms and left for Japan."

Four women and four men will represent the United States as Pluhowsky, now 43, will be joined by fellow Ohioan Jillian Martin, Illinois resident Gianna Brandolino and Jordan Snodgrass, of Michigan.

The men's team also has Miami Valley ties as Chris Via, of Springfield, will compete with Andrew Anderson of Michigan, Texas native Ryan Barnes and Packy Hanrahan of Kansas. The eight Team USA bowlers have collected nearly 140 combined medals — Pluhowsky has tallied 50 medals in Team USA competition including four golds in Hong Kong in 2011 alone.

Via, 34, will be making his eighth Team USA appearance.

"When you are competing for your country, you feel like you have a little more to give," he said.

The men's team also has a bit of unfinished business after a ninth-place finish at the 2025 International Bowling Federation World Championships in Hong Kong in December. The women's team won the team title and were the overall women's federation champions.

"We want to be the best in the world, so leaving empty handed was definitely disappointing," Via said. "But I think that will help us moving forward and we can learn from it."

Junior Team USA: Mission accomplished for 2026 Bellefontaine High School graduate Gracie Hood.

The incoming Mount Mercy University bowler — who finished as runner-up at the U.S. High School Bowling National Championship — recently qualified for Junior Team USA at the 2026 Junior Gold Championships. Six boys and six girls — including the top four U18 male and female bowlers after 26 games of qualifying — were named to the team. Hood was in second place after qualifying.

She expressed her aspirations just weeks earlier after wrapping up the national high school event.

"I want to make Team USA," Hood said at the time. "That's my goal."

title: 🏆 Who will win the Ballon d'Or? Mbappé, Rodri, Kane, Dembélé or... 🃏
description: Who will win the Ballon d'Or? A question we’ve almost always had a ready answer for over the last 15 years. And if there were already people turning up their noses at Dembélé’s win in the 2024/2...
fullText:
Who will win the Ballon d'Or? A question we’ve almost always had a ready answer for over the last 15 years. And if there were already people turning up their noses at Dembélé’s win in the 2024/2025 season, this year there will be plenty of unhappy people (almost everyone).

Because this season there hasn’t been one player clearly above the rest, or else — if there has been — he hasn’t managed to win almost anything. The balance between performances, club trophies and international success has never been this hard to figure out.

Zero trophies this season at Real Madrid. That alone would normally be enough to rule him out of the Ballon d'Or. And that’s how it seemed until June 16: then France began its World Cup and Kylian Mbappé started planting that doubt in our minds, with his goals, his fire and the composure of a champion under Paraguay’s kicks.

In short, a World Cup worthy of an absolute champion: 10 goals in the competition, 22 in total, smashing Klose’s record and pulling clear of Messi (who we’ll talk about later). But if we take a step back, what do we remember from Mbappé’s season with Real Madrid? 42 goals, 15 of them in the Champions League.

Ballon d'Or numbers. And yet the trophy count stays at zero: did Real fail because of Mbappé, or did they come close thanks to him? There’s truth in both sides of that coin. And Luis Enrique would probably answer the same way, since on the day Mbappé left he said: "Today I will be a very rainy day, but it is also a beautiful day because you can feel a different kind of air". But what kind of air? The same one Spain breathed throughout the World Cup.

If you’re looking for a self-made candidate (like the others, but starting from farther back), who then became a leader through experience, capable of scoring in ways he never thought possible and of battling throughout his career against the label of “loser”, then Harry Kane is your man. Oh, and he also scored 73 goals this season.

6 of those came at the World Cup, 2 of them in a true Royal Lion finish against DR Congo. Then came another goal against Mexico, one that will go down in history not only for its importance but for what he himself said about it: practically inaudible in the post-match interview, broken by everything he no longer had — energy, emotion. He probably lip-synced "Wonderwall", but oh well.

As for trophies, it was an excellent yet imperfect year. Bundesliga won in dominant fashion, German Cup won, Champions League semifinals, and 3rd place at the World Cup (above Mbappé). Could he have done more? But really... What else?

Winning a Ballon d'Or without setting foot outside the US — World Cup qualifiers aside — would be ironic, and yet it’s possible because Lionel Messi is allowed feats beyond the ordinary. And sometimes even beyond what’s "fair", though that concept is debatable.

His MLS season was close to perfect: goals in bunches, assists galore, and a league title. But that matters little — or should matter little — for the Ballon d'Or. What matters is his World Cup: central, in every sense.

For Argentina, because his touch was on 12 of their 18 goals in the entire tournament (8 goals and 4 assists). In public opinion: "Mbappé has scored. What will Messi do?" In the eyes of opponents and even in the controversies: definitely overblown, but not entirely baseless. Favoritism? I don’t think so. Reverence? Definitely yes.

The final was the dividing line for the next Ballon d'Or, and it probably spared us from seeing it handed to a phenomenon who played 8 real matches in a year. Le Roi est mort, but where is the next one?

That sense of deep admiration, and at the same time the absence of wonder, might suggest: "Another Ballon d'Or for Dembélé?" It could happen, because the current holder of the trophy showed up when PSG really needed him: 6 goals and 1 assist in the last 5 Champions League matches, including a penalty scored in the final. Let’s not forget it — that ball weighed a ton even through the screen, and he put it in the net.

He won the Ligue 1 MVP award in a way that made no sense: 10 goals in 22 matches, because Luis Enrique rests his stars in the league. In the Champions League, though, he wasn’t the best: Kvartskhelia took the award, rightly so. But he missed the World Cup and so seems out of the running: the world is unfair.

At the World Cup, we saw the same old limits and flashes again: he’s not the guest star, but he’s no longer a supporting player either. He dominated when he could, and faded when he had to carry the weight of an imperfect machine like France. Deschamps is not Luis Enrique, and the Ballon d'Or feels that most of all... maybe he’s not the favorite to win it again.

Let’s take the Dembélé argument and flip it: the amazement Yamal inspires is the main reason to give him the Ballon d'Or. That’s why he finished 2nd last year. That’s why he could finish 1st this year. Because last season he won LaLiga and improved every number with Barça: more goals and more assists despite playing significantly less, without giving up the spectacle.

But that magic and efficiency weren’t enough to go further in the Champions League. And above all, they sagged at the World Cup: few superstar plays, just 1 goal, just 1 assist. And maybe it’s not even his fault, because that muscle issue right before the tournament may have cost him the Ballon d'Or. To convince France Football, you need everything: pragmatism, sparkle, trophies and iconic moments. Yamal only lacked the last of those.

Rodri’s season can be summed up like this: a long run-up to the World Cup. Minutes in his legs, confidence building in his head, some muscle problems, a few dips (especially in crucial Champions League matches) and 2 trophies with Manchester City: the FA Cup and the EFL Cup. Not too bad, without Djokovic’s irony.

What was extraordinary was his World Cup: Spain’s metronome went back to setting the tempo with fluency and without mistakes, helping La Roja slowly boil their opponents, who gradually lost everything: possession, confidence, the ability to attack and, finally, the match itself. Spain’s victory was like Rodri’s game: not very flashy, gradual and inevitable. Few saw it at the start, no one disputed it at the finish line.

But is that enough for the Ballon d'Or? If absolute quality and international trophies are what matter, yes. If the whole season is judged, absolutely not. Rodri does, however, have the same advantage that allowed him to win in 2024: there are many candidates in the running, and back then Vinicius and Bellingham took votes off each other. This time, that effect will be even stronger.

This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇮🇹 here.

title: Seahawks plan at running back is bad news for Jadarian Price
description: This plan for Seattle could lead to a lackluster rookie year for Jadarian Price.
fullText:
Seahawks plan at running back is bad news for Jadarian Price originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Seattle Seahawks are heading into their 2026 training camp with a few interesting questions to resolve, most of which come on the offensive side of the ball.

New offensive coordinator Brian Fleury will be a topic of conversation, but the Seahawks will also have a few position groups that are worth paying close attention to. Of those positions, running back is the most interesting.

As ESPN's Brady Henderson highlighted, the Seahawks plan at the moment with their running back room does not bode well for Jadarian Price in his rookie year.

"The Seahawks are high on George Holani and, by all appearances, plan to give him a good chunk of the work alongside No. 32 overall pick Jadarian Price," Henderson writes.

This plan for the Seahawks, to utilize George Holani in an expanded role alongside Price, won't help the first-round rookie put together a strong debut season in the NFL.

Price was the backup for Jeremiyah Love at Notre Dame, and now, while he most likely won't be the backup, he's still seemingly going to split playing time with Holani.

With Charbonnet potentially coming back later in the season too, Price might not have much time, if any at all, as the featured back in this RB room.

MORE: Seahawks trade idea lands Jackson powers-Johnson from Raiders for one draft pick

He should see plenty of opportunity early in the season, but with the Seahawks being high on Holani and planning to involve him in the offense, Price might have a bit of a lackluster rookie season.

Of course, he could prove he deserves more work by thriving in training camp and preseason. But if Price doesn't thrive right away, Holani could take over a larger share of the backfield in 2026.

This running back plan for the Seahawks doesn't exactly bode well for Price, as he might not see as much work as Walker did when he was the lead back with Charbonnet out and Holani as the backup.

title: Toppin returns to Dayton for sixth annual basketball camp
description: Jul. 23—Obi Toppin has added drivers, irons and a putter to a bag of tricks that includes his signature between-the-legs EastBay dunk. The former Dayton Flyers forward, now preparing for his seventh season in the NBA, held the Obi Open on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, at NCR Country Club. The event benefited the Toppin Family Foundation, which he founded earlier this month. Toppin's mom, Roni ...
fullText:
Jul. 23—Obi Toppin has added drivers, irons and a putter to a bag of tricks that includes his signature between-the-legs EastBay dunk.

The former Dayton Flyers forward, now preparing for his seventh season in the NBA, held the Obi Open on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, at NCR Country Club. The event benefited the Toppin Family Foundation, which he founded earlier this month. Toppin's mom, Roni Toppin, is the president of the foundation.

The foundation's mission, according to its website, is to "create opportunity where it is needed most — supporting underprivileged children and families while investing in student-athletes who face financial hardship. We believe access to support, education, and athletics can change lives and strengthen our community for generations to come."

Toppin said they created the foundation to help the next generation. He had some struggles as a kid and wants to give kids a "better path to their dreams."

The golf outing was one of the foundation's first fundraising events. Toppin still shares many more basketball photos than golf photos on his Instagram profile, but the game is growing on him.

"My golf game is getting better every day," Toppin said. "It's all right. I can hold my own. I'm not the best golfer, but I'm getting better every single day."

Toppin, the consensus national player of the year in his final season at Dayton in 2020, wasn't the only former Dayton All-American on the course. DaRon Holmes II, who arrived at Dayton one year after Toppin departed, also played in the event.

"He told me it was his first time on a golf course," Toppin said. "I'm like, 'No way.' So I've got to get out there with him more and teach him golf."

Holmes signed autographs and posed for photos with members of the Flyer Faithful a day earlier at Lee's Famous Recipe Chicken in Kettering.

Obi Toppin: A tribute to a new Dayton Flyers hall of famer

Toppin returned to Dayton not only for the golf outing but for his sixth annual basketball camp. The Obi Toppin Basketball ProCamp, sponsored by CareSource, welcomed approximately 150 kids in grades one through eight to the University of Dayton RecPlex on Thursday. The camp continues Friday.

Toppin started the camp at the Bales Center in Beavercreek in 2021. In 2022, it moved to UD, where it has been held ever since.

"It's amazing," Toppin said. "I see a lot of familiar faces. I feel like it gets better and better every year, and it looks like there are more kids than previous years."

Toppin planned to head straight from the camp Thursday to speak to Dayton's current players at the Cronin Center. He met some of the players earlier in the week at a dinner.

Toppin could speak to the players about any number of experiences he's had in his career, including overcoming injuries. A foot injury marred his sixth NBA season and third with the Indiana Pacers. He played in the first three games of the season but didn't return to games until Feb. 26. He then appeared in 21 of the last 23 games.

One season after losing to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the NBA Finals, the Pacers finished with the second-worst record in the NBA and the worst record in franchise history (19-63). The team's star player, Tyrese Haliburton, missed the entire season after tearing his Achilles tendon in Game 7 last summer.

"There were a lot of injuries last year, but we have a healthier team this year," Toppin said. "We've got a great group of guys coming back, and I feel like our group has a really good chance."

The struggles of the Pacers coincided with the rise of Toppin's former team, the New York Knicks, who won the NBA championship one year after being eliminated by the Pacers in the Eastern Conference finals.

"I'm obviously close to the guys on that team, so I'm super happy for them," Toppin said. "They worked really hard to get to where they had to get to."

Now Toppin's goal is for the Pacers to follow the Knicks as champions.

"We've got to go get us one," Toppin said.

title: Report Lucumi price tag set at €25m after Besiktas proposal rejected
description: Reports suggest Bologna turned down a Besiktas offer for Jhon Lucumi, consisting of €12m plus Jean Onana, with an asking price of €25m for the defender also linked with Inter, Juventus and Sunderl...
fullText:
Reports suggest Bologna turned down a Besiktas offer for Jhon Lucumi, consisting of €12m plus Jean Onana, with an asking price of €25m for the defender also linked with Inter, Juventus and Sunderland.

The Colombia international had a release clause in his contract set at €28m, but that expired a few days ago.

Considering his current deal expires at the end of the season and Bologna confirmed they had promised to let him leave if a suitable offer arrived, his exit seems fairly inevitable this summer.

MEXICO CITY, MEXICO – JUNE 17: Jhon Lucumi #3 of Colombia looks on during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match between Uzbekistan and Colombia at Mexico City Stadium on June 17, 2026 in Mexico City, Mexico. (Photo by David Ramos/Getty Images)

Tuttomercatoweb and Sporx both claim that a proposal came in from Besiktas worth €12m cash plus ownership of midfielder Onana, who spent last season on loan at Genoa.

However, Bologna are only prepared to accept the full cash sum for this sale, so no player exchanges will be considered.

The €25m asking price has now been set and could include add-ons, so there is no shortage of interested teams.

Besiktas do have an advantage, in that he already worked with coach Vincenzo Italiano at the Stadio Dall’Ara.

MONZA, ITALY – SEPTEMBER 22: Jhon Lucumi of Bologna FC in action during the Serie A match between Monza and Bologna at U-Power Stadium on September 22, 2024 in Monza, Italy. (Photo by Francesco Scaccianoce/Getty Images)

Lucumi just turned 28 last month and represented Colombia at the 2026 World Cup.

title: Packers Discussion: What’re you looking for early on in training camp?
description: What new information are you hoping to have by this time next week?
fullText:
The Green Bay Packers will get back to work next week, even though they won’t be in full pads. Here’s my question for you today: What information are you looking for in these early training camp practices?

Here are a couple of things I will be paying attention to:

On paper, everyone should be back other than Micah Parsons, Jordon Riley and Tucker Kraft (who should be good to go pretty early in camp) based on the injury recovery timelines we know about. If guys like Zach Tom, Devonte Wyatt and Kamal Hadden aren’t back out there, my ears are going to be poking up.

Here are just some assorted things I want to keep an eye on roster-wise:

Is Pierre Strong Jr. or Damien Martinez getting in the mix with the 1s at all? If the Packers lose a ball-carrier, whoever has the edge there is probably going to make the 53-man roster.

Is Y tight end Luke Lachey getting looks with the 1s? After Kraft, Lachey might be the second-best option as an in-line blocker that the team has right now. Maybe he steals a roster spot (or the team keeps four at the position).

How is the defensive line rotation working? We still aren’t sure who will be the third starter next to Wyatt and Javon Hargrave…or who will be playing nose tackle in odd fronts.

Is anyone stepping up across from Lukas Van Ness? Early on in the season, one of Barryn Sorrell, Brenton Cox Jr., Collin Oliver or Danie Dennis-Sutton will be starting at outside linebacker. It’s anyone’s guess who ends up winning that battle.

What does the cornerback rotation look like, assuming Benjamin St-Juste is back healthy? St-Juste missed minicamp practices with an undisclosed injury, giving all those opportunities to Carrington Valentine and Brandon Cisse opposite Keisean Nixon. How much of a chance does St-Juste have, really, to win the job?

Everything special teams. Is Skyy Moore doing enough? Who is starting on which units? Is Trey Smack playing well? All of it.

Let us know what you’re going to track in camp in the comments section.

title: The 'Tarps Off' Trend Explained
description: Over the last two months, huge swaths of baseball stadiums have been taken over by fans spinning their shirts over their heads, but this phenomenon goes back further than you might think. Here's how the "Tarps Off" trend started and swept the sports world.
fullText:
One of the biggest fan traditions to emerge from the 2026 baseball season didn't start with a mascot or a chant. Instead, it involved fans ripping off their shirts and spinning them over their heads in an attempt to fire up the stadium during big moments.

Known as the "Tarps Off Trend," the celebration craze has spread across college football, Major League Baseball (MLB) and other sporting events, turning shirt-twirling into a viral rallying ritual.

While most people have probably only heard of this phenomenon in the last couple of months, the trend goes back much further and riffs on the slang term "tarp," referring to a shirt, that's been around even longer.

So, where did this trend originally start, and how did it become one of the defining sports fan crazes of 2026? Let's explain.

The phrase "tarps off" is slang for going shirtless, but the viral sports trend largely traces its origins to an Oklahoma State (OSU) football game back in October 2025.

During the game, fan Trent Eaton reportedly accepted a $10 bet from his sister to enter the student section, remove his shirt and wave it over his head.

A TikTok video of Eaton enthusiastically twirling his shirt while surrounded by an otherwise subdued crowd quickly went viral. As more fans joined in, the moment became the blueprint for what would later become known as the "Tarps Off" trend.

Over the following months, similar displays appeared at college football games across the United States before making the jump to professional sports and the MLB.

The movement gained renewed momentum in the spring of 2026 after fans at a Colorado Rockies game adopted the celebration, while the phrase "tarps off" itself also became increasingly common in sports clips shared online.

The trend reached a new level of popularity in May 2026 when members of the Stephen F. Austin State University baseball team attended a St. Louis Cardinals game at Busch Stadium.

Attempting to energize the crowd during a comeback, the players removed their shirts and spun them over their heads, helping spark an atmosphere that culminated in a Cardinals walk-off victory.

Videos of the celebration quickly spread across social media in May, prompting the Cardinals to embrace the movement by inviting the "Tarp Crew" to meet the team and later designating an official shirt-optional fan section in the stadium.

Throughout the summer of 2026, the tradition spread to stadiums across Major League Baseball, with fans at games involving the Tampa Bay Rays, Seattle Mariners, Detroit Tigers, Kansas City Royals, Houston Astros and other teams adopting the celebration.

The trend also inspired commentary videos, memes and parodies, including posts showing women participating, such as one shared under the "Women in Male-Dominated Fields" meme format, cementing "Tarps Off" as one of the summer's biggest sports trends.

For the full history of the Tarps Off trend, be sure to check out Know Your Meme's encyclopedia entry for even more information.

title: UFC Vegas 120 fight card revealed – including Darren Elkins return
description: A member of the UFC roster since 2010, Darren Elkins will step into the cage for promotional bout 31.
fullText:
Darren Elkins is giving UFC appearance No. 31 a go.

The lineup for UFC Fight Night 284 was officially announced by the promotion Thursday, with Elkins (29-12 MMA, 19-11 UFC) among the new bout additions. The event takes place Aug. 8 at Meta APEX in Las Vegas.

Elkins, 42, will battle rising Cuban up-and-comer Yadier del Valle (10-1 MMA, 2-1 UFC) in a three-round featherweight bout. He has not competed since an April 2025 knockout loss to Julian Erosa. Despite his longevity and seasoned status, Elkins had won back-to-back fights prior to the loss. Elkins made his UFC debut in March 2010, making him one of the longest-tenured fighters on the roster.

Del Valle, 29, looks to bounce back from the first loss of his professional career. After he started his promotional tenure with back-to-back first-round submission wins over Connor Matthews and Isaac Dulgarian, del Valle lost a unanimous decision to Jordan Leavitt in February.

title: ‘I’m Aiming For A Knockout’ – Ryujin Nasukawa Promises A Debut Fans Won’t Forget At The Inner Circle 23
description: Confidence meets aggression in this kickboxing war that promises fireworks in Lumpinee Stadium on July 24.
fullText:
ISKA World Champion Ryujin “Shindo” Nasukawa has made it clear he wants to lead kickboxing’s next era, and that mission gets underway at The Inner Circle 23 in Bangkok’s Lumpinee Stadium this Friday, July 24.

The 20-year-old Japanese phenom opens his account in the world’s largest martial arts organization against Thai slugger Thongpoon PK Saenchai in a strawweight kickboxing showdown. The card will be available exclusively to subscribers at live.onefc.com.

Stepping onto the global stage for the first time carries a weight that no amount of preparation can fully simulate.

Every athlete who has walked this same path, regardless of pedigree or hype, has had to face that same unknown the moment fight week finally arrives. That reality is no different for Ryujin, who is the younger brother of now-retired undefeated Japanese kickboxing great Tenshin Nasukawa.

Confidence has defined nearly everything he has said leading up to this moment, but even the most self-assured fighters admit there is a human side to a debut this significant.

“It still hasn’t really hit me yet. I honestly won’t know whether I’ll be nervous or not until fight day actually comes.”

Those nerves, whenever they arrive, will not catch Ryujin unprepared.

Thongpoon’s style is no secret to anyone who has followed his run inside ONE Championship, and the young gun has put in the hours breaking down exactly what he brings into the ring.

That preparation has left him with a clear, specific read on his opponent’s approach.

“He comes forward relentlessly and doesn’t seem to care about taking punches to get in close.”

That read on Thongpoon’s aggressive style has only sharpened the Chiba native’s own intentions heading into the biggest chapter of his career yet.

True to the explosive reputation that built his 18-3 record in the first place, Ryujin has no interest in simply getting his hand raised. He wants to announce himself with a debut that will long live in the memory of fight fans everywhere.

“I’m aiming for a knockout. I want people to think, ‘Something crazy just showed up.’

“I want a finish that goes beyond what everyone’s expecting. I want to bring a fresh wind to ONE and to the combat sports world as a whole.”

A statement finish in his promotional debut would be only the beginning of what Ryujin Nasukawa hopes to accomplish in ONE Championship.

The 20-year-old has spent his career chasing gold across multiple promotions, having claimed an ISKA World Title and becoming a two-division RISE Champion along the way. And he has no intention of slowing down now that he has arrived on the grandest stage for kickboxing.

Rather than treat strawweight as the ceiling, he sees it as just the starting point of something much bigger.

“I really want to win, leave a strong impact, and show that I have what it takes to chase World Titles in both the strawweight and atomweight divisions.”

While Friday marks his first promotional appearance as a competitor, it is far from his first taste of life inside the world’s largest martial arts organization.

Ryujin sat in the corner for fellow countrymen Sora Tanazawa and Kazuteru Yamazaki at ONE Friday Fights 158 and The Inner Circle 18 this past June, and he witnessed how electric the atmosphere can be at Lumpinee Stadium.

Now, he gets to experience that same energy firsthand, with the hope of one day competing at events like ONE SAMURAI as well.

“Everyone was so fired up. It didn’t matter which fighter won or lost, the crowd was electric [during the show last month]. It really made me realize what an intense stage this is.

“ONE SAMURAI is shining a spotlight on the Japanese stage, and for that, I’m really grateful on behalf of Japan’s combat sports scene. I think it’s a card that’s going to keep building momentum.”

title: Casemiro breaks silence amidst MLS investigation into alleged ‘tampering’ against Inter Miami
description: Inter Miami's newest signing, Casemiro, has spoken out on his decision to join the Herons, with an investigation launched into the team by the MLS regarding alleged 'tampering'
fullText:
Wednesday’s announcement that five-time UEFA Champions League winner Casemiro had joined Lionel Messi and Co. at Inter Miami ended up being somewhat overshadowed by a statement made mere minutes later by Major League Soccer that an investigation into the Herons had been launched.

In the statement, the MLS explained that it was “reviewing a tampering allegation against Inter Miami CF. The league is gathering all relevant information and will refrain from further comment until the review is complete.

“While Inter Miami CF and the LA Galaxy have reached a settlement for the Discovery priority to sign Casemiro, the terms will be released upon conclusion of the tampering investigation.”

Donald Trump sparks outrage with Harry Kane World Cup comments

Argentina World Cup Final red card rescinded, face potential FIFA disciplinary action

While Miami has yet to address the investigation, so far declining to comment after being contacted by All Out Soccer, the Galaxy did state, “The LA Galaxy and Inter Miami CF have reached a settlement for the Discovery priority to sign Casemiro, and the terms will be released upon conclusion of the tampering investigation currently open with Major League Soccer.”

Since then, Casemiro, who joins after four seasons in the Premier League with Manchester United, has made his position crystal clear that it was Miami or bust as far as his MLS dreams were concerned.

“I’d like to clarify the situation - especially regarding the talk about me - by saying that I spoke with the LA Galaxy a while back, but I made it very clear to them that I wanted to play for Inter Miami,” the 34-year-old said, via online translation.

“It’s a city I love, and a place my family and children know well since we’re always here whenever we have days off. So, from the moment I spoke with the Galaxy, I made it absolutely clear that the only team I wanted to play for in MLS was Inter Miami.”

The Brazilian international, who recently started in every game during their run to the FIFA World Cup Round of 16, added, “The project and the excitement of playing alongside some of the greatest players of all time - that’s a huge motivator.

To listen to the latest episode of 'All Out Soccer' - CLICK HERE

“I’m incredibly grateful to Inter Miami for making the effort to bring me here; I wanted to be here, too. As I said, Inter Miami was the only place in MLS where I wanted to be. I want to express my thanks and show that on the pitch.

“I’m eager to get started and show it through my play. I’m really excited to reciprocate the affection I’ve received from everyone - the club, the owners, Jorge [Mas], [David] Beckham, and of course Leo and the other players. I just want to channel that excitement onto the field and, naturally, help my teammates.”

While unable to play in Miami’s first post-World Cup game as he continues to wait on a visa, Casemiro will have been watching on closely as his new team returned to action by collecting three points at Nu Stadium against Chicago Fire FC on Wednesday. Luis Suarez scored twice as the Herons, still led by interim boss Guillermo Hoyos, spoiled Robert Lewandowski’s day as he made his debut for the Fire.

Miami will return to action on Saturday when they travel to Canada to take on CF Montreal, before returning to Florida to host the Columbus Crew a week later.

title: Jets relying on Cade Klubnik to avoid 'bummer' result for backup QB competition
description: This QB competition could be a "bummer" if Cade Klubnik does one thing.
fullText:
Jets relying on Cade Klubnik to avoid 'bummer' result for backup QB competition originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The New York Jets don't exactly have a ton of optimism for the 2026 season as a postseason contender. But there are plenty of individual talents worth getting excited about.

Just not likely at the quarterback competition. At least, outside of Cade Klubnik. The rookie fourth-rounder from Clemson has a chance to compete for the backup QB job in New York this summer, and this is a more important competition than it might seem.

As Rich Cimini of ESPN notes, the Jets are relying on Klubnik to win the QB2 job behind Geno Smith in 2026 to avoid a "bummer" outcome of relying on Bailey Zappe or turning to some other veteran.

"Rookie Cade Klubnik is the fan favorite because ... well, the success-starved fan base is desperate for a QB of the future," Cimini writes. "No offense to Bailey Zappe, but it would be a bummer if he wins the No. 2 job."

While the Jets could turn to Brady Cook as the QB2, all signs are pointing towards the competition for the backup job going to either Klubnik or Zappe.

Ideally, the Jets can confidently declare Klubnik as the backup quarterback after training camp and the preseason. If he wins that job, then there could be some real optimism for the Jets at the QB position.

MORE: Dolphins trade idea sends Jaylen Wright to Texans for late-round draft pick

Even if he doesn't turn into the future franchise QB, just having a young player who has some upside would be a breath of fresh air for the Jets franchise.

That's why Klubnik winning the backup QB job in 2026 is so important. If it's Zappe, it's a sign that the Clemson QB is not the answer, let alone a player that fans can have optimism for.

This QB competition might not draw the same attention as some others around the league, but if Klubnik earns the QB2 job, then the fanbase can have something to look forward to during the season.

title: More than 400 days out: De Jong’s sorry injury record at Barcelona as he faces another 5-6 months out
description: Frenkie de Jong’s latest injury adds another chapter to an increasingly frustrating fitness record since arriving at FC Barcelona in 2019. As confirmed by the club today, the Dutch midfielder has be...
fullText:
Frenkie de Jong’s latest injury adds another chapter to an increasingly frustrating fitness record since arriving at FC Barcelona in 2019.

As confirmed by the club today, the Dutch midfielder has been diagnosed with a tear of the medial collateral ligament in his right knee, ruling him out for five to six months and dealing Hansi Flick a major setback ahead of the 2026-27 season.

Signed from Ajax as one of Europe’s brightest midfield talents, De Jong has seen his Barcelona career repeatedly interrupted by injuries.

Over the past seven years, he has suffered multiple muscular problems, a previous knee injury and three serious injuries to his right ankle, all of which have affected his continuity for both club and country.

In all, Frenkie has been out for more than 400 days through injuries during his time at Barcelona, and will now miss another 150 days, taking the total to well over a year and a half.

De Jong’s first significant injury as a Barcelona player came in June 2020, when he suffered a calf muscle injury in his right leg during training.

The setback forced him to miss the closing stages of the season and sparked debate over the management of his recovery.

The following campaign also proved difficult. During pre-season, he sustained a calf injury but recovered in time for the start of the competitive season.

Later that year, another muscular problem sidelined him for several matches, and in January 2022 he picked up a minor muscle strain that briefly kept him out again.

The 2022-23 season brought further setbacks. While on international duty with the Netherlands in September, De Jong suffered another muscle injury that forced him to miss several Barcelona matches.

In March 2023, he then sustained a knee injury that ruled him out for around a month, causing him to miss important fixtures in both La Liga and the Copa del Rey.

De Jong’s most difficult period came during the 2023-24 season.On 23 September 2023, he suffered a syndesmotic sprain in his right ankle during a league match against Celta Vigo.

The injury kept him out for more than two months and forced him to miss 14 official matches.

Just as he appeared ready to return, he suffered another injury to the same ankle against Athletic Club on 3 March 2024, delaying his comeback once again.

De Jong has had a tough time due to injuries. (Photo by David Ramos/Getty Images)

The worst blow arrived on 21 April 2024 during El Clasico at the Santiago Bernabeu as it resulted in a third sprain to the same ankle in less than a year. It was the most serious injury of his Barcelona career to that point and sidelined him for more than five months.

The lengthy recovery also meant De Jong missed Euro 2024 with the Netherlands after it became clear his ankle had not recovered as hoped. It marked one of the biggest disappointments of his professional career.

His recurring ankle problems prompted discussions about surgery, but both the player and Barcelona ultimately opted for conservative treatment in an effort to avoid an operation.

Although the rehabilitation process was lengthy, it eventually allowed him to return to competitive football.

Despite those setbacks, De Jong fought his way back into Barcelona’s starting lineup and re-established himself as one of the team’s most influential midfielders under Hansi Flick, proving once again the level he can reach when fully fit.

He endured a couple more spells out on the sidelines during the 2025/26 season, one due to a muscle problem, another because of a hamstring injury.

Now, another serious injury has interrupted his progress. With a knee ligament injury set to keep him out for an extended period, Barcelona will once again have to wait for one of their key players to complete another difficult road to recovery.

title: Insider Drops Mets-Clay Holmes Extension Update
description: The New York Mets are going to be sellers at the trade deadline and Clay Holmes could be on the move
fullText:
The New York Mets' postseason chances are near zero, and they could be the most active seller at this year's trade deadline.

They have plenty of rental arms teams could be interested in, including Clay Holmes. The Mets signed him to be a starter after he found success as a reliever with the New York Yankees. In his first year as a starter, he had a 3.53 ERA. This year, he had a 2.39 ERA through nine starts before landing on the injured list. He fractured his fibula after taking a combacker off the leg.

"At this point, an extension between right-hander Clay Holmes and the New York Mets ahead of the Aug. 3 trade deadline appears unlikely, multiple major-league sources say," MLB insider Robert Murray wrote on Thursday. "Things can change with one phone call, especially at this time of the year, so nothing can be totally ruled out. But Holmes is expected to draw significant trade interest in the coming days as he’s getting closer to returning from a fractured fibula that’s sidelined him since mid-May."

Holmes is expected to make his first rehab start on Thursday, and many teams will have scouts there to watch him. He has a $12 million option for next season, and considering how well he has performed as a starter, he could be interested in testing the open market.

Looking for the perfect gift for a baseball player in your life? Check out our brand new baseball lifestyle brand!

The Mets would be wise to deal him for prospects in a lost season. He gets a ton of ground balls, and he could thrive on a team with an elite infield. The Milwaukee Brewers and Chicago Cubs would both make sense as landing spots for him.

More MLB: Yankees Reportedly Considering Calling Up Top Prospect For Debut

title: Dave Roberts Comments on Eric Lauer’s Undefeated Record
description: The Los Angeles Dodgers are yet to lose when starter Eric Lauer pitches for them, currently boasting an 8-0 record when he pitches.
fullText:
The Los Angeles Dodgers are yet to lose when starter Eric Lauer pitches for them, currently boasting an 8-0 record when the left-hander pitches.

The Dodgers traded for Lauer after the Toronto Blue Jays put him on waivers in May, and he has been a big part of a bullpen which suffered some injuries early in the season. Through his eight appearances, Lauer has a 3.35 ERA and a spotless 4-0 record.

Lauer is aware of the streak he has put together, and has let manager Dave Roberts know about his performances while showing off the confidence he’s grown.

“He’s told me,” Roberts said. “He’s told me that when he starts, we win.”

Lauer notoriously spoke out against having an opener while he played for the Blue Jays, and used his solid record with the Dodgers as leverage when the Dodgers proposed the idea. The Dodgers still went through with using an opener, having Will Klein come out before Lauer threw six no-hit innings against the Minnesota Twins.

“That was when we were discussing the opener that we used the one time (in Minnesota last month), and I just mentioned to him (Roberts) I was like, ‘Listen, we’re 4-0 when I start the game,’” Lauer said of his interaction with Roberts. “But it was more of a joke than anything.”

The southpaw pitched against the Philadelphia Phillies on Wednesday, and turned in yet another solid performance in his long list of strong showings for the Dodgers. Despite allowing three runs through 5.1 innings pitched, Lauer kept the Dodgers in the game and allowed them a chance to win. He struck out five batters in the game, matching his high with the Dodgers.

For the time being, Lauer has certainly earned his keep as a starter for the Dodgers—especially with their current injury crisis. When players start returning, though, he may make a move into the bullpen.

The Dodgers have a five-man rotation for the time being, as nobody has replaced Shohei Ohtani since the Dodgers shut him down from pitching due to his irritated knee. Blake Snell is on his way back, so he would likely fill the open slot whenever he does come back, and Ohtani would force someone out of the rotation.

That player could be Lauer depending on the team’s view on right-hander Emmet Sheehan, but the Dodgers also have a decision to make with Lauer before Aug. 3. Fabian Ardaya of The Athletic says the Dodgers could trade him at the trade deadline if their starters come back from the injured list.

“Depending on how Snell and Glasnow progress through the end of July, trading Eric Lauer — especially with several teams looking to fortify their rotations — remains a possibility,” Ardaya wrote.

While the future isn’t certain for Lauer at the moment, his strong performances are more than welcome in the Dodgers rotation for as long as he continues producing.
