title: Ukraine's ousted defense minister rejects Zelenskyy's offer of a different government role
description: Ukraine’s fired defense minister said Thursday he has rejected President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s efforts to persuade him to rejoin the government in a different role where he could continue fighting Rus...
fullText:
KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's fired defense minister said Thursday he has rejected President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's efforts to persuade him to rejoin the government in a different role where he could continue fighting Russia's more than 4-year-old invasion.

Mykhailo Fedorov, who was dismissed last week after just six months in the job, is a youthful and popular public figure due to his success in modernizing the armed forces and giving Ukraine an edge in the war through innovative use of technology. He has also cracked down on corruption in military procurement.

His departure triggered street protests across Ukraine that have put pressure on Zelenskyy and cheered Moscow officials.

Fedorov said in a statement that leading the Defense Ministry was one of only three jobs that "actually shape the course of the war," along with the presidency and chief of the armed forces.

He said that he would "not accept any position other than" defense minister, in what amounted to a defiant stance against Zelenskyy.

The Ukrainian president said earlier Thursday he had offered Fedorov the position of deputy prime minister for military innovation.

Fedorov said he wanted to finish his reforms at the Defense Ministry, which were cut short after his relationship soured with Soviet-trained commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi, who was viewed as part of the military old guard.

"No other role carries the actual authority to combat procurement corruption, complete the army's transformation, plan and execute asymmetric operations against the enemy, root out a culture of lies and unaccountability within the system," he said of the Defense Ministry post.

He said he took over last January with a plan to end the war by improving air defenses and holding firm on the front line while hurting Russia's economy through long-range strikes.

"Our collective results created something unprecedented, a window of opportunity to force Russia into peace through strength. And we cannot afford to lose it," he said.

Follow the AP's coverage of the war in Ukraine at https://apnews.com/hub/russia-ukraine

title: Jensen Huang made his $172 billion fortune on AI chips. His family’s $75 million gift to Vanderbilt argues art decides what technology is for
description: The $75 million gift to establish an art, architecture and design college is the largest Huang has ever made to a university.
fullText:
Jensen Huang has been on a bit of a philanthropic streak, having donated one of his iconic leather jackets to raise nearly $1 million for the Edge Institute, a nonprofit that brings together people working in tech, science, culture, and society to live together in pop-up villages and work on experiments.

What concerns exist about the CCA-Vanderbilt transition?

What happened to California College of the Arts?

Why did Nvidia's CEO donate to an art school?

How is Vanderbilt expanding its university presence nationally?

Now the Nvidia CEO and his wife, Lori, are making a somewhat unexpected turn, donating $75 million to Vanderbilt University for its art, architecture, and design San Francisco campus. It might seem odd for one of the world's most prominent technology leaders to donate to an art school, but the Huangs have interesting reasoning.

"Technology expands what we can build. Art and design determine why we build it," Huang said in a statement. "Together they shape civilization."

Pending approval, the gift will establish the Jen-Hsun and Lori Huang College of Art, Architecture and Design, which is "envisioned as a hub" for creatives, according to Vanderbilt. The goal is to establish a school focused on advanced tech breakthroughs, art and architecture, developing technical and visual mastery through studio practice and design labs, and also to help students build both business and tech fluency. The gift has already spurred more than $25 million in additional donations, for a total investment topping $100 million so far.

With that context, it makes a little more sense why Huang, CEO of a $5 trillion AI chips company would be invested in the future of art and tech convergence. And it isn't his first move here. The San Francisco campus is taking over the site of the California College of the Arts, the 119-year-old institution that will graduate its final class in 2027 after struggling with falling enrollment and a roughly $20 million deficit. Huang had backed CCA before its collapse, according to the San Francisco Standard. His combined giving to CCA and Vanderbilt now approaches $97.5 million, according to the Standard, making the Vanderbilt gift his largest ever to a university, topping the more than $50 million he has given his alma mater, Oregon State.

San Francisco is also the logical home for the school, as opposed to Nashville, where its flagship university sits, because it puts students at the epicenter of new technology development.

"This college, together with additional academic programming under development, will prepare creative leaders to shape culture, industries and communities in San Francisco's thriving economy, nationally and around the world," Chancellor Daniel Diermeier said in a statement.

title: Nolan Wells’ second autopsy results ‘hampered’ by several factors: Expert
description: Dr. Michelle DuPre said the second autopsy is somewhat difficult because it comes after someone's already examined the body.
fullText:
(NewsNation) — After the news that a private autopsy found Nolan Wells' cause of death inconclusive, a former medical examiner says the results were impacted by several factors.

Preliminary findings show the Mississippi teen's cause and manner of death are undetermined and require further investigation, according to a report by Roger Mitchell, the president of the National Medical Association.

Wells' family decided to pursue their own autopsy after the discovery of their son's body a few days after he went on a trip with friends to an island off the Mississippi coast.

Dr. Michelle DuPre told "CUOMO" on Wednesday the second autopsy is somewhat difficult because it comes after someone's already gotten a hold of the body.

"In this case, the autopsy results are equivocal," she said, noting the red discoloration around the back of Wells' head, no case of fractures or deep tissue injuries, and the decomposed body.

Wells' body had missing neck structures and organs during the second autopsy, which DuPre says is very important to make a determination of possible strangulation.

"I understand why the original pathologist has kept this to look at it further, but it does hamper the second autopsy and doesn't give real results," she stated.

Authorities said they had no suspicion of foul play being involved in Wells' death. However, Mitchell, who did the private autopsy, hasn't ruled it out.

DuPre called it "premature" that authorities came to such a conclusion so quickly.

"Until we do an investigation, we simply don't know," she said. "One of the things we want to avoid is getting that tunnel vision."

The Mississippi Medical Examiner's Office has completed its own autopsy but hasn't released results.

NewsNation's Josie Fischels contributed to this report.

title: Spain's Zapatero says seized jewels worth $1.5 million were a 'courtesy gift'
description: MADRID, July 23 () - Spanish former Prime Minister Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero said on Thursday that €1.5 million) of jewellery seized by ‌police as part of an investigation was a personal gift he ‌r...
fullText:
MADRID, July 23 (Reuters) - Spanish former Prime Minister Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero said on Thursday that €1.3 million ($1.5 million) of jewellery seized by ‌police as part of an investigation was a personal gift he ‌received years ago and was never intended for financial gain.

Zapatero, who was prime minister from 2004 ​to 2011 and remains an influential figure in the governing Socialist Party, is under investigation over alleged influence-peddling, accusations he denies. The case is one of a series of corruption scandals affecting the party and piling political pressure on Prime ‌Minister Pedro Sanchez's government.

In his ⁠first interview since being placed under investigation two months ago, Zapatero told Spanish public broadcaster TVE that the jewels found ⁠during a search of his office were "a personal courtesy gift" that had been stored for years with other family valuables.

The jewels were unrelated to his political activities ​and their ​origin and handling would be fully ​explained before a judge, he said. ‌He rejected suggestions they reflected any attempt at personal enrichment.

Investigating Judge Jose Luis Calama opened a separate probe into the jewellery, whose origin remains undocumented, according to investigators. An auction house provisionally valued them at about €1.3 million ($1.5 million), though Zapatero disputes that estimate.

Zapatero said that, with hindsight, he might not have ‌accepted the gift.

"Looking back, I would have ​probably taken another decision," he said.

The jewellery is ​one strand of a wider ​investigation that also includes allegations that Zapatero profited from using ‌his influence to secure the state-backed ​rescue of airline Plus ​Ultra. He also denies those allegations.

The opposition People's Party (PP) dismissed Zapatero's comments on Thursday.

PP spokesperson Ester Muñoz said Zapatero "has every right to lie, ​but he has not ‌answered any of the questions or doubts Spaniards had" about the case.

title: Chaos erupts as House passes $1.15T defense bill with Israel provision: 'Stop stoking Jew hatred'
description: A bipartisan coalition fought Section 219's U.S.-Israel defense cooperation provision, but the $1.15 trillion NDAA still cleared the House Wednesday.
fullText:
The House passed its $1.15 trillion annual defense policy bill Wednesday after a bipartisan revolt over a provision expanding U.S.-Israel defense technology cooperation.

The fiscal year 2027 National Defense Authorization Act passed 216-212, with Republicans largely supporting and Democrats mostly opposing the measure.

The annual bill, which sets Pentagon policy and typically draws broad bipartisan support, received only six Democratic votes as much of the caucus objected to Section 219 and Republican-backed amendments restricting transgender care and easing access to personal guns on military bases.

House Democrats Fracture Badly Over Massie Amendment To Cut $3.3B In U.s. Aid To Israel

Opposition came from a coalition of progressive Democrats and Republican skeptics of U.S. support for Israel, who accused congressional leaders of shielding the provision from scrutiny after lawmakers were denied a separate vote to remove it.

The fight erupted as support for Israel has weakened among House Democrats and some "America First" Republicans during the war in Gaza and the widening conflict with Iran. Just one week earlier, 103 Democrats joined Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky., in voting to block $3.3 billion in U.S. aid to Israel.

For opponents, Section 219 would move the relationship in the opposite direction by embedding U.S.-Israel cooperation more deeply inside the Pentagon's weapons-development and acquisition system. The provision would direct the war secretary to designate a Pentagon "executive agent" to coordinate and accelerate bilateral defense research, testing and industrial cooperation.

Critics argued that structure would make the relationship harder for future administrations or Congresses to unwind and could shift parts of the partnership away from highly visible aid votes into less visible technology, procurement and production arrangements. Supporters said it would coordinate longstanding programs and give the United States faster access to Israeli defense technology.

The Us-israel Strategic Alliance – Partnership, Not Charity

Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., urged lawmakers to reject the entire defense bill, arguing that the provision would "merge parts of our military with the IDF," referring to the Israel Defense Forces.

"This amendment is an existential threat to American sovereignty and democracy," Ocasio-Cortez wrote on X prior to the vote, referring to Section 219. "Every member of Congress must vote NO."

Rep. Mike Lawler, R-N.Y., shot back on X, "Good grief, you are either obtuse or intentionally lying. We are not merging our military with the IDF. As we do with many of our allies, we enter into cooperative agreements, share intelligence, develop technology and ammunitions, and conduct military exercises together. Stop stoking Jew hatred and do better."

Massie similarly opposed both the rule governing debate and the final bill after Republican leaders denied a floor vote on his bipartisan amendment to strike the provision.

"We should not merge our military tech and supply chain with Israel's," Massie said on X.

Beyond creating the executive agent, Section 219 would direct the Pentagon to identify jointly developed or Israeli-origin technologies that could be used in U.S. weapons programs and promote joint ventures, licensing agreements and U.S.-based production partnerships with Israeli companies.

Before final passage, the House adopted two amendments from Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, R-Fla. One removed four uses of the word "integration," while the other extended annual reports to Congress for as long as the executive agent remains in place. The original reporting requirement would have ended in 2030.

The amendments left the initiative's central structure and cooperative programs intact, falling short of opponents' demand that Section 219 be removed entirely.

Sen John Fetterman Pledges To Be 'Last Man Standing' In The Democratic Party In Support Of Israel

Massie's amendment was backed by Democratic Reps. Ro Khanna of California, Jim McGovern of Massachusetts, Jesús "Chuy" García of Illinois and Rashida Tlaib of Michigan.

Section 219 would not combine the countries' command structures, place U.S. troops under Israeli authority or give Israel control over U.S. military operations. The United States and Israel also already cooperate extensively on defense technology, including the Iron Dome, Arrow and David's Sling missile-defense systems, counter-drone technology and anti-tunnel capabilities.

The provision's principal change would be to place a designated Pentagon executive agent in charge of coordinating and expanding those efforts across the War Department.

House Armed Services Committee Chairman Mike Rogers, R-Ala., said the provision would improve oversight and efficiency by assigning responsibility for existing initiatives to a single official.

"In no way does it give away command and control of our military operations, personnel or equipment," Rogers said last month.

Critics argued that the executive agent structure would give U.S.-Israel projects a privileged path through the Pentagon and further entrench Israeli technology and companies in the American defense supply chain.

The fight threatened the broad coalition traditionally needed to pass the NDAA, one of the few major bills Congress has routinely approved with bipartisan support. Democratic opposition was also fueled by Republican amendments restricting gender-affirming medical care for troops and military dependents and transgender participation in sports at Defense Department schools.

The House voted 219-208 Tuesday to prohibit Tricare from covering certain gender-transition procedures and treatments, with five Democrats supporting the amendment and one Republican opposing it.

Lawmakers separately voted 221-203 to prohibit transgender girls from participating in girls sports at War Department schools.

The House rejected a separate attempt by Rep. Lauren Boebert, R-Colo., to codify President Donald Trump's executive order barring transgender troops from serving in the military. That amendment failed 217-212 after four Republicans joined every Democrat in opposition.

Lawmakers also narrowly approved an amendment making it easier for qualified service members and civilian War Department employees to receive permission to carry personal guns on military installations. The measure passed 215-214.

An amendment that would have largely prohibited Ukraine security assistance failed, though 76 Republicans supported it. The proposal would have preserved funding only for security at the U.S. Embassy in Kyiv.

The underlying NDAA would authorize roughly $1.15 trillion for national defense, including pay raises ranging from 5% to 7% for service members, expanded weapons and munitions production and new investments in missile defense, autonomous systems, hypersonics and other emerging technologies.

The bill would also formally change the Department of Defense's name to the Department of War, deeming references to the defense secretary and department in federal law to mean the secretary and Department of War.

The NDAA sets defense policy and authorizes spending levels but does not provide the money itself. Congress must separately pass defense appropriations legislation to fund the programs and priorities included in the measure.

Consideration of the legislation had been delayed for three weeks by a separate Republican fight over the SAVE America Act, Trump-backed election legislation requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote and identification to cast a ballot in federal elections.

The House approved the election measure in February, but it stalled in the Senate, where Republicans lacked the 60 votes needed to overcome a filibuster.

Republican leaders revived the legislation through a procedure directing the House clerk to combine it with the NDAA after passage but before sending the package to the Senate. That approach allowed the House to vote on the defense and election measures separately before transmitting them as one bill.

The plan initially collapsed June 30 when 13 Republicans joined Democrats in voting against the procedural rule. Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, R-Fla., and other holdouts later relented after Speaker Mike Johnson promised to attach the election legislation to appropriations bills and other must-pass measures.

The combined defense and election package faces an uncertain future in the Senate, where Democrats blocked consideration of their chamber's NDAA last week in protest of the Trump administration's war with Iran.

The debate also comes as the Pentagon seeks additional money to cover the conflict and replenish U.S. weapons stockpiles. The White House has requested an $87.6 billion supplemental package, including $67.1 billion for defense needs, while House Republicans are considering additional military and intelligence funding through a party-line reconciliation package.

Original article

title: Why Mark Cuban says many AI data centers could wind up as 'pickleball courts'
description: Mark Cuban joked that AI efficiency gains could lead to obsolete data centers being turned over to pickleball fans.
fullText:
Mark Cuban joked that pickleball players could be unlikely winners from the AI buildout.

He quipped that if better AI makes some data centers obsolete, they could be turned into courts.

Cuban said the AI boom isn't another dot-com bubble but could "destroy" VCs, funds, and PE firms.

Mark Cuban joked that the AI buildout could result in pickleball fans having plenty more venues to choose from.

The tech billionaire said during the latest episode of the "All-In" podcast that if AI models and data centers become more efficient over time, much of the computing capacity being built today will become redundant.

The former "Shark Tank" investor said that so-called hyperscalers like Meta and Alphabet, which are collectively pouring hundreds of billions of dollars into building data centers, are correctly anticipating that AI usage will rise over time.

But he predicted that "technological breakthroughs" will make AI cheaper and more power-efficient. He pointed to how there was a similar rush to build fiber networks, but eventually connection speeds accelerated, and there "wasn't a bandwidth problem anymore."

Cuban said he couldn't see how we "don't get the same price-performance improvements on the AI side and on the data center side."

If he's right, there will be "a lot of data centers that are going to be turned into pickleball courts," he quipped. Cuban is the co-owner of the Dallas Flash pickleball team.

The Broadcast.com founder, who sold his internet-radio startup to Yahoo for $5.7 billion in April 1999, sounded the alarm on Big Tech companies plowing cash into AI infrastructure, borrowing heavily to fund those outlays, and pledging to keep spending for years to come.

"That's planning for perfection, and that's going to be hard," Cuban said, adding that "nobody can predict that well."

Cuban said the AI boom isn't as extreme or wide-reaching as the internet bubble, limiting its blast radius if it does blow up.

"It's not the traditional dot-com bubble, right?" Cuban said. He pointed to fewer companies going public today at "crazy valuations" and seeing their stock prices double despite having "no revenue, no traffic, no nothing" — and fewer cab drivers chatting about their favorite internet startups to every passenger who will listen. Cuban said that "you don't see that at all today."

"So, it's not a bubble that's going to impact most people in the room, or most people across the US, but it could just destroy a lot of VCs and a lot of funds and a lot of PE, right?" he continued. "Because they're going all in."

 /

 Katrina vanden Heuvel and John Nichols

Trump’s personalization of foreign policy is ultimately a symptom of a deeper institutional problem rather than its source.

I left America in 2017 to flee an authoritarian government and the culture it facilitated. What I found in Berlin was not all that different.

title: Nolan’s “Odyssey” Misses the Point
description: Katha...
fullText:
The big-budget movie keeps much of the violence and spectacle but none of the nuance and wit of the classical epic.

Odysseus was my hero when I was a child. I loved that he was a wanderer, an adventurer, a teller of tales, but most of all I loved that he was really really smart—polutropos, “the man of many wiles.” He got himself into and out of tight spots by being clever, by talking, paying attention, looking ahead, making a plan. The other Greek heroes, Heracles, for example, were just big oafs waving swords. I barely noticed that Odysseus did plenty of his own sword-waving. He wasn’t just brain; he was brawn too. I just took what I needed as a small female person who loved writing poetry and hated gym.

You could say Christopher Nolan did the same, just taking what he needed, in his three-hour all-star, all-IMAX film of Homer’s epic. Guilt and trauma have long been themes of his work (Oppenheimer, Memento), so why not make Odysseus a traumatized veteran of the Trojan War, obsessed with guilt for his crimes against humanity? After all, he did come up with the stratagem of the Trojan Horse, which, after 10 long years of stalemate, allowed the Greek army to enter the walled city and slaughter its desperate, screaming inhabitants—a horrific scene presented by Nolan several times in flashbacks. But I’m not sure the ancient Greeks were as tender-hearted as we are (or pretend to be). Certainly, there is no suggestion in Homer that Odysseus was troubled by his part in the war. His very first deed on the way home was to lead his men on a raid of the Troy-allied island of Ismaros, where they stole everything not nailed down, killed the men, and enslaved the women. (It ends badly, but only because his men lack Odysseus’ prudence and hang about feasting while the inhabitants regroup and attack.) Think of Odysseus as a kind of Viking—he plunders both for treasure and for glory.

Nolan skims over that, along with much else. He doesn’t tell us that after the killing of 108 suitors, Odysseus forces the slave women who’ve consorted with them to clean up the carnage and then directs his son Telemachus to kill them. Telemachus, blandly played in the movie by Tom Holland, goes his father one better: Those sluts don’t deserve an honorable death by the sword, so he hangs them (“they gasped, feet twitching for a little while, but not for long,” in Emily Wilson’s translation). Telemachus also cuts off the nose, ears, genitals, hands, and feet of the disloyal goatherd Melanthius. If Odysseus is a Viking, Telemachus has the makings of a first-class serial killer.

The omission of these savageries isn’t what troubles me about Nolan’s version—a modern audience would be revolted by them, and it’s not like the film lacks blood and gore and cruelty. It’s that he cuts out so much that is funny, beautiful, witty, and poetic. We have the Cyclops episode, but not shrewd Odysseus directing his men to grasp on to the wooly underbellies of the Cyclops’s sheep so the now blind giant won’t feel them as they escape the cave. We don’t have possibly the world’s oldest dad joke when the Cyclops asks for the name of the man who blinded him and Odysseus shouts “No One” causing the giant to bellow out “No one hurt me!” to the great bewilderment of the other giants. We don’t meet the charming, peaceful Phaiacians and their adorable teenage princess Nausicaa. We don’t hear the Sirens’ song. We do have Odysseus’ seven-year dalliance with the beautiful goddess Calypso (Charlize Theron)—but Nolan has her secretly drugging Odysseus with lotus flowers so that he forgets about his past life, including his beloved wife, Penelope. Calypso is like a New Age sex healer, prone to pronouncements like “Give yourself to the storm.… give up control and live.” And indeed the two of them look very Californian, taking long walks on the beach wearing billowy, sand-colored robes. In Homer’s poem, Odysseus knows perfectly well that he’s married, and weeps on the beach every day from homesickness.

A more effective modernization is the Circe episode. In Homer, Circe, who enjoys turning men into animals, for some reason, is a beautiful goddess in a lovely palace, with whom (after forcing her to free his men from their transformation into pigs) Odysseus has a year-long mutually delightful affair. Nolan makes her a weary, middle-aged Wiccan in a tumbledown stone cottage, who turns his men into pigs because “this is who they are.” Preach, sister! Samantha Morton is absolutely brilliant in the part—just about the only actor in the film who comes across as the character she is playing and not a movie star playing a part. Anne Hathaway is a passionate and angry Penelope, but mostly she is Anne Hathaway dressed in shimmering blue gowns and elaborate gold jewelry. You never forget that Odysseus is Matt Damon.

Much has been written and tweeted attacking Nolan’s decision to have a gorgeous Black actress, Lupita Nyong’o as Helen of Troy. It’s true that she is “white-armed Helen” in the poem, but come on, people! You should be used to nontraditional casting by now. You could even say that making Helen Black is a way of showing how different she is from everyone around her, being half divine and born from a swan’s egg and all. What bothered me was the scene where Telemachus, looking for news about his father, visits her and her husband, Menelaus, in their palace at Sparta. In the film, Menelaus is a brute and a wife-beater, who has horribly scarred Helen’s face. In Homer, Helen and Menelaus are generous and sophisticated hosts, seemingly untouched by all they have been through. Like Tom and Daisy Buchanan in The Great Gatsby, they’ve caused immense damage that has no effect on them, rather like the Homeric gods themselves.

Taking out this material leaves us with a simpler, more sentimental story of a man who just wants to go home, who loves his old dog, his son, and his wife, and somehow ends up killing an enormous number of people. He doesn’t seem particularly clever or wily and he’s anything but complicated (Emily Wilson’s translation of polutropos). He’s basically yet another action hero—big-biceped, strong, sweaty, taciturn. Nolan leaves out the plot points most alien to contemporary sensibilities—the extreme violence and sadism, the gods constantly interfering in human affairs. (Only Athena, played by Zendaya, appears now and then, mostly to criticize him, less a majestic deity than a girlfriend who is really tired of picking up his socks.) But he also leaves out much that makes Homer’s Odyssey an indelible work of imagination, Odysseus an endlessly fascinating character, and his story so rich in multiple meanings—so polutropos—that people have been turning it over in their minds for three thousand years.

title: Indiana basketball vs. Virgin Islands start time, streaming info
description: Indiana basketball plays the Virgin Islands on Thursday night. Here's how to watch the Hoosiers play in the FISU America Games:
fullText:
BLOOMINGTON — Indiana basketball will face the Virgin Islands in the FISU America Games at 6 p.m. on Thursday night. Both teams won their first game in the group stage of the event with lopsided victories over Peru.

The Hoosiers are representing the USA in the event that brings together college athletes from North, Central and South America and the Caribbean for a competition across 15 sports. The participating teams in basketball include Chili, Colombia, Argentina, Brazil, Virgin Islands, Peru and Team USA. The top two teams in each pool advance to the elimination rounds on Saturday.

Watch Indiana basketball play in the FISU America Games

Indiana's games throughout the event will stream on the FISU American Games' YouTube page. There is no separate streaming or radio option. Standings for the event can be found on the event's website.

Indiana forward Aiden Sherrell led his team with 19 points (7-of-11) and 10 rebounds, and Prince-Alexander Moody added 17 points. The Hoosiers had a total of seven players score double digits while shooting 56.1% from the field as a team.

Aiden Sherrell: 19 points, 10 rebounds, two blocks

Prince-Alexander Moody: 17 points, three rebounds, three assists

Vaughn Karvala: 13 points, seven rebounds

Trevor Manhertz: 11 points, four rebounds, four assists

Jaeden Mustaf: 11 points, six rebounds

Markus Burton: 10 points, four rebounds, six assists

Trent Sisley: 10 points, six rebounds

July 23: Indiana vs. Virgin Islands; 6 p.m.

Michael Niziolek is the Indiana beat reporter for The Bloomington Herald-Times. You can follow him on X @michaelniziolek and read all his coverage by clicking here.

This article originally appeared on The Herald-Times: Indiana basketball vs. Virgin Islands start time, streaming info

title: Jon Sumrall is the bluntest talent evaluating coach ever
description: Want a blunt evaluation of your NFL Draft stock? Jon Sumrall seems like the type that would give it to you.
fullText:
First year Florida head coach Jon Sumrall does not sugarcoat it. If you want an accurate assessment of where you stand as a player, how good or bad you are, Sumrall could likely tell you. Want a blunt evaluation of your NFL Draft stock? Sumrall seems like the type that would give it to you.

It's no secret that Florida will be building their offense around talented tailback Jadan Baugh, one of the top running back prospects in in the class of 2027, this season. The Gators are breaking in a new QB1, and according to Sumrall, he won't have to exactly set the college football world on fire with his play, this season.

"None of them need to be Tim Tebow," Sumrall said at SEC Media Days, alluding to the man who won a Heisman Trophy and two national championships in Gainesville.

"They need to be somebody that just manages a game and operates the right way at the line scrimmage and doesn't turn the football over. We've got decent skill players around the quarterback position with Jadan Baugh and the receiver group."

In saying "game manager" and "hey, just don't turn the ball over," Sumrall is basically saying "you don't have to be good, but just don't be awful."

Georgia Tech transfer Aaron Philo is said to have the slight upper hand in the QB competition right now. However, he faces a stiff training camp position battle against second year Gators QB Tramell Jones Jr.

Sumrall expounded more on the quarterback situation:

"Do we have a Heisman Trophy candidate? No, but they both have enough arm talent. They both are adequate runners. Neither one of them are elite runners. Neither one of them are slugs either. Both have good arms, both have good releases. Both can make all the throws. So there's enough there for us to win with."

Again he makes the QB room sound pretty mid right now.

Sumrall is going viral right now for his faking outrage over a reporter's cellphone going off in the middle of his media session.

"You'd be kicked out of our team meeting ... You're lucky I'm not gonna throw this freakin' podium." 😅A phone went off during Jon Sumrall's press conference 😬 pic.twitter.com/0Xsy7sball

— SEC Network (@SECNetwork) July 22, 2026

However, that sequence may have actually been the least interesting thing that he said on Wednesday.

This quote, about his upbringing in Alabama, has the internet buzzing: “I grew up thinking there were three leagues: the AFC, the NFC and the SEC."

While it's meant as a compliment to the league where "it just means more," it also just serves a joke right up on a silver platter. Or at least it does for those who might be cynical about how the SEC may have operated in the pre-NIL era.

This article originally appeared on Draft Wire: Jon Sumrall is the bluntest talent evaluating coach ever

title: Yankees Rotation Could Be Set Up To Carry Team To World Series
description: The New York Yankees Rotation is in a great spot
fullText:
The New York Yankees finally have Max Fried back, and their rotation is set up for a deep postseason run.

Between Cam Schlittler, Max Fried and Gerrit Cole, the Yankees could throw out an ace in every game if they roll with a three-man rotation in October. Will Warren or Ryan Weathers could serve as a fourth member if needed, but that top three is more than capable of getting the job done.

Schlittler is the favorite to win the American League Cy Young Award this year, and would likely start Game 1 of any series. Behind him is Cole, who has won a Cy Young Award and just delivered three straight quality starts. Finally, there is Max Fried, who, in his return from the injured list, went five innings, allowing just one hit and striking out seven batters.

Offense is still something the Yankees need to fix, but in October, pitching matters more, and the Yankees have a starting rotation capable of putting up zeros on a nightly basis. The bullpen is playing well right now as well.

Looking for the perfect gift for a baseball player in your life? Check out our brand new baseball lifestyle brand!

David Bednar, Fernando Cruz, Brent Headrick and Paul Blackburn have all become weapons for Aaron Boone. Angel Chivilli has dominated in his nine outings this year as well. All of a sudden, the Yankees' pitching staff as a whole looks like their biggest asset.

When Aaron Judge and Giancarlo Stanton return, the Yankees will have a complete team, capable of winning a relatively weak American League.

More MLB: Mets Superstar Heating Up As Trade Rumors Continue

title: These PGA stars face pressure as FedEx Cup playoffs loom
description: Keegan Bradley and Brian Harman are among 22 stars fighting to keep their playoff hopes alive at the 3M Open.
fullText:
BLAINE, Minn. — As Yogi Berra once said, it's getting late early on the PGA Tour.

Only three weeks remain in the regular season of the FedEx Cup; only 70 players move on to the playoffs. Heading into this week's 3M Open, Keegan Bradley and Brian Harman are among the 22 players that made the playoffs last season and are currently outside the top 70 of the 2026 FedExCup standings. Bradley, who ranks 77th, made it all the way to East Lake and the Tour Championship and had done so three straight seasons, finishing 16th while Harman, currently No. 78, wasn't far behind him at No. 18. Both are in the field this week at TPC Twin Cities and looking to improve their position. Another player on the list, Denny McCarthy, who enters the week at No. 97, summed up the challenge ahead: "Gotta play good golf now, no ifs ands or buts about it." Here's a look at the full list of the 22 on the outside looking in.

Adam Schupak is a senior writer for Golfweek, covering the PGA Tour.

This article originally appeared on Golfweek: These PGA stars face pressure as FedEx Cup playoffs loom

title: How many touchdowns will Seahawks QB Sam Darnold throw in 2026?
description: How many touchdowns will Seattle Seahawks quarterback Sam Darnold throw in 2026?
fullText:
Sam Darnold established himself as a franchise quarterback for the Seattle Seahawks during the 2025 season. Darnold was a high-level performer en route to the organization winning Super Bowl 60. The former USC standout will now attempt to repeat that success in 2026 while defending his Lombardi title.

Darnold threw for an effective 4,048 yards during the campaign. He threw for 25 touchdowns and 14 interceptions. Darnold's 2026 goals will include a similar amount of passing touchdowns while eliminating some turnovers.

The Seahawks have undergone significant changes on offense. Brian Fleury is the new offensive coordinator, replacing Klint Kubiak. The team also lost Super Bowl 60 MVP Kenneth Walker III to free agency, replacing him with rookie Jadarian Price, and a possible increased workload for George Holani (until Zach Charbonnet is back healthy).

Coach Fleury was once a run-game expert for Kyle Shanahan and the San Francisco 49ers. That would possibly indicate he'll prefer to lean on the ground game. With so much uncertainty at running back, though, the Seahawks will need Darnold to operate a well-balanced offense.

The Seahawks remain deep at wide receiver, with reigning Offensive Player of the Year Jaxon Smith-Njigba leading a position that also has Cooper Kupp and Rashid Shaheed. Darnold should throw for another 25+ touchdowns, with the upside for a 30+ TD season. Darnold appears primed for another big season in Seattle.

This article originally appeared on Seahawks Wire: How many touchdowns will Seahawks QB Sam Darnold throw in 2026?

title: Will Commanders' defensive overhaul be effective in 2026?
description: The Washington Commanders completely revamped their defense during the offseason, but are those changes going to be effective in 2026?
fullText:
Adam Peters had one major goal during the offseason: Make the Washington Commanders' defense younger and faster. He addressed that largely in free agency, signing players like Odafe Oweh, K'Lavon Chaisson, and Leo Chenal, to name a few. He added to that list in the draft by selecting Sonny Styles with the No. 7 overall pick and edge rusher Joshua Josephs in the fifth round.

Suffice it to say that between the departures and additions, the Commanders' defense looks very different in 2026 than it did in 2025. But will what Peters did actually pan out this season? Under new defensive coordinator Daronte Jones, one of the biggest storylines is whether this defense can get all of its new faces on the same page, something that NFL.com's Grant Gordon wrote about earlier this week.

When you fall from being everyone's NFC Championship Game darling to a 5-12 struggle bus, you can run out of fingers to point. The Commanders put a massive UNDER CONSTRUCTION sign in front of a defense that finished dead last in yards allowed. They used the No. 7 overall pick on Ohio State linebacker Sonny Styles, an athletic marvel who will draw plenty of hype as a preseason Defensive Rookie of the Year candidate. General manager Adam Peters opened the check book before turning in the card to get Styles, too. Washington broke the bank to sign pass rusher Odafe Oweh, while adding plenty more veteran starters elsewhere: nickel Amik Robertson, safety Nick Cross, pass rusher K'Lavon Chaisson, linebacker Leo Chenal and defensive tackle Tim Settle. The Commanders added veteran depth, as well. Binging on free agents isn't always a winning formula, of course. How quickly new defensive coordinator Daronte Jones and Washington's cast of fresh faces get on the same page will be crucial in camp.

If you paid attention during minicamps and OTAs, you already know that the defensive players are putting a huge effort into creating bonds off the field just as much as on the field. It's not a secret that the bond between teammates is equally as important as the skill set each player brings to the table.

The biggest problem the Commanders will have here is the number of new faces on the roster. It's one thing if you bring in a handful of new players to join an otherwise stable unit, but it's another beast altogether when you add a dozen or more new faces. This is the task ahead of Daronte Jones: Make this unit a cohesive group that functions as such.

Jones has an advantage in this, though. The Commanders' defensive players want to bond. They want to get to know each other. They want to work together. They want to win games and prove that they weren't just slapped together haphazardly. Getting the players to buy in is half the battle, and these guys are already there. Now Jones just has to get them to understand his scheme and execute plays proficiently.

This article originally appeared on Commanders Wire: Will Commanders' defensive overhaul be effective in 2026?

title: Prescott, Cowboys have to break even-numbered year jinx
description: Dak Prescott has had his best seasons during odd-numbered years, the Dallas Cowboys need him to break the even-numbered injury jinx in 2026
fullText:
It wasn’t a banner season for the Dallas Cowboys in 2025. Despite the offense’s success, the team finished under .500 for the first time in a season where franchise quarterback Dak Prescott started at least half of the games.

2025 was one of Prescott’s best seasons in his 10-year career, however, which made sense since it was an odd-numbered year. Never being able to string two great seasons together, It’s in the even numbered years that the team’s QB has struggled, mostly because he hasn’t been able to stay healthy this decade. If the Cowboys want to compete for a Super Bowl, Prescott must avoid the injury bug and play well in a calendar year that ends with an even number.

This has been a seven-season pattern with Prescott, who has excelled in the odd numbered years. In the last four, Prescott has thrown for at least 30 touchdowns, had his four highest passing yardage years and has maxed out at 11 interceptions. Prescott’s averages in the last four odd-numbered seasons are 279.4 passing yards per game, 33.25 TDs, and 10 interceptions, statistics that would have put him top 5 for passing yards and touchdowns in each of the last four seasons.

He’s also missed just one game in those four seasons.

In his last three even-numbered seasons, Prescott has suffered two season-ending injuries, failed to throw for over 23 scores and led the NFL in interceptions once. There was some bad luck in Prescott’s league-leading 15 picks in 2022, but he didn’t play his best ball that year, either.

In 2020, there was the dislocated ankle, followed by a broken thumb that caused him to miss five games in 2022. A torn hamstring in 2024 saw Prescott miss the final nine games of a season that wasn't going well.

The organization has tied so much to Prescott that it’s hard for the team to survive when he goes down for an extended length of time. Dallas did win in 2022 with backup Cooper Rush starting five games but won just eight games combined when Prescott was out for the season in 2020 and 2024.

With a healthy Prescott in 2025, the Cowboys’ offense was one of the best in the league and the veteran QB was in the top 5 for passing yards, ypg game and passing scores. It’s a unit that can get even better if their top wide receiver tandem of CeeDee Lamb and George Pickens play all year together. The offensive line should improve with Tyler Guyton and Tyler Booker heading into their second and third seasons, as well.

The running game with Javonte Williams should also continue to be a strength after finishing ninth in the league in rushing last year.

Prescott will be 33-years old for the 2026 season, but his play in 2025 showed no indication that he’s slowing down. When healthy, Prescott is a top QB in the game.

One of the best abilities in the NFL is availability, and Prescott hasn’t been on the field enough in the even-numbered seasons recently to give the Cowboys a chance. That cannot be the team’s trend for 2026. Prescott needs to find a way to play in a majority of the team’s games this season.

If that doesn’t happen, the Cowboys can forget all the pre-season Super Bowl talk. Let the curse of the even year die for Prescott and the Cowboys in 2026.

This article originally appeared on Cowboys Wire: Prescott, Cowboys have to break even-numbered year jinx

title: UNC basketball offers intriguing guard in 2028 class
description: The UNC basketball program has offered four-star combo guard Brady Pettigrew.
fullText:
As North Carolina continues to put together its recruiting board for the 2027 class, head coach Michael Malone and the staff are identifying some top targets.

With the AAU cycle officially in the books, the next steps in recruiting will be Fall visits. North Carolina already has a few visits lined up from some top prospects, but they are hoping to get more. On Wednesday, they officially entered the race for another recruit in the 2028 class.

Four-star combo guard Brady Pettigrew took to social media to announce that North Carolina is the latest team to offer him.

After a great conversation with coach Malone, I’m blessed to receive an offer from The university of North Carolina!!!🩵🤍 pic.twitter.com/bhV7yVY2v2

— Brady Pettigrew (@BradyPettigreww) July 23, 2026

The combo guard hails out of Bolingbrook, Illinois and plays at Bolingbrook High School. He has 33 offers in his recruitment, including from Illinois, Michigan, Arizona State, Arkansas, BYU, Florida, and Tennessee, among other programs.

The 6-foot-3, 185-pound combo guard is ranked No. 19 overall, No. 1 combo guard, and the No. 1 player in the state of Illinois per the 247Sports recruiting rankings.

title: Yordan Alvarez competing for MLB Triple Crown after hitting 34th home run in Astros' 5-2 win over Marlins
description: Alvarez leads MLB in two of three Triple Crown categories, and all three in the AL.
fullText:
With 58 games remaining in the Houston Astros' 2026 season and two months left on the schedule, it might be too early to talk about Yordan Alvarez potentially winning a Triple Crown.

Yet after Alvarez hit his 34th home run of the season, it's not unreasonable to raise the possibility either. The Astros slugger batted 2-for-4 with 2 RBI in Houston's 5-2 win over the Miami Marlins.

Alvarez now leads MLB in homers, overtaking the Philadelphia Phillies' Kyle Schwarber. And he has four more than Ben Rice of the New York Yankees for the top spot in the AL.

With 77 RBI, Alvarez is one ahead of the Washington Nationals' CJ Abrams and Jordan Walker of the St. Louis Cardinals. And though he trails the Marlins' Otto Lopez (.332) for the MLB lead in batting average, his .328 mark leads the Tampa Bay Rays' Yandy Diaz (.308) by 20 points.

MLB's last Triple Crown winner was the Detroit Tigers' Miguel Cabrera, who led the AL with a .330 batting average, 44 homers and 139 RBI. But as might be expected, Alvarez doesn't want to talk about the Triple Crown right now.

"I'm just focused right now on playing ball," Alvarez said to MLB.com's Brian McTaggart. "There's more than two months remaining in the season, so I'm not going to talk anymore about the Triple Crown. I don't want to answer any more questions about the Triple Crown."

With the Astros fighting for a playoff spot, Alvarez is understandably reluctant to discuss individual achievements. Not to mention that talking about a thing makes it a thing that could present an obstacle to peak performance. Wednesday's win improved Houston's record to 50-54, two games behind the first-place Texas Rangers (51-51) in the AL West and 1.5 back from the Seattle Mariners (51-52) for second place. The Astros face more of an uphill climb in the AL wild-card race, 3.5 games from the league's third spot with four teams ahead of them — including the Boston Red Sox, who just finished a 15-game winning streak.

The Astros have won three consecutive games after sweeping the Marlins. However, they lost seven of 10 before that, including two of three to the Rangers, the team Houston is trying to catch in the AL West.

That skid raised questions about the Astros being sellers at the trade deadline, perhaps dealing off players like shortstop Jeremy Peña, third baseman Isaac Paredes or reliever Josh Hader. Yet Houston could just as easily be buyers at the deadline, looking to add pieces, including a starting pitcher, high-leverage reliever or outfield bat to provide a boost to its playoff chances.

"You get swept, you sweep somebody back, right?" said Astros manager Joe Espada, via MLB.com.

"That's a good bounceback win. That was good stuff," he added. "We bounced back very well against a good Marlins team. Beat one of the best pitchers in the game. I think our players, our team reacted very well after the way we played against Baltimore."

The Astros face another test this weekend with a three-game set at the Chicago White Sox (54-47).

title: ‘Whoever Wins, Come Face Me’ – Prajanchai Breaks Down Interim World Title Clash Between Sam-A And Aliff
description: Prajanchai offers his prediction for the massive showdown this Friday at The Inner Circle 23.
fullText:
Reigning ONE Strawweight Muay Thai World Champion Prajanchai PongSuphan PK has a deeply personal investment in Sam-A Gaiyanghadao‘s blockbuster showdown with Aliff Sor Dechapan in the main event of The Inner Circle 23 this Friday, July 24.

Whoever walks away on top will be crowned the ONE Interim Strawweight Muay Thai World Champion, and with it, a future clash with Prajanchai. The card broadcasts live in Asia primetime, exclusively on live.onefc.com, from the historic Lumpinee Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.

Prajanchai’s connection to this matchup runs deep, forged through his own history with both men. He ushered in his first reign by dethroning Sam-A via majority decision at ONE: BATTLEGROUND in 2021. The Thai tactician delivered a far more decisive verdict two years later at ONE Friday Fights 22, knocking out the legend in the second round to claim interim gold.

Earlier this year, the Bangkok native was expected to defend his crown against Aliff, and the pair even shared a heated staredown to promote the looming war. However, the match never materialized after Prajanchai required surgery for a lingering injury, which ultimately paved the way for this interim title clash instead.

That unresolved chapter aside, a different kind of battle now awaits, one where a stark 20-year age gap has many favoring the younger man.

Aliff is also chasing the distinction of becoming Malaysia’s first-ever ONE World Champion, a quest already backed by Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim.

Still, Prajanchai insists it would be unwise to count out Sam-A. The ageless veteran is riding a red-hot three-fight winning streak against considerably younger opposition, fueling the case for a possible trilogy down the line.

“In this matchup, I view Aliff as the favorite over Sam-A because he’s younger and fresh, plus he has the advantage in terms of speed and reach.

“However, you can’t underestimate Sam-A. Even though he’s older, he remains dangerous. Recently, even when he gets knocked down, he always gets back up. And in his latest fight, he even knocked out a younger opponent. His physical condition is truly remarkable.”

Anticipation continues to build around this striking masterclass, a clash of styles pitting youth and blistering speed against decades of hard-earned experience.

That reality has left Prajanchai genuinely torn on an official prediction, content instead to embrace the role of a curious spectator for this high-stakes battle of eras.

“Both Sam-A and Aliff are incredible fighters, so this fight is bound to be exciting. I believe that unless someone makes a mistake first, it will definitely go the distance.

“Regardless of who wins, I’m ready to defend my belt against either of them. Whoever wins, come face me.”

Prajanchai PongSuphan PK has been sidelined since early 2026, but the Thai appears to be closing in on a long-awaited return to action.

The PK Saenchai Muaythaigym representative has attacked his rehabilitation with the same relentless intensity and dedication he brings inside the ring, and that grueling work is paying off as he nears a full recovery.

“Right now, my physical condition is back to around 90 percent, and I’ve resumed normal training. I continue to train every day to stay fit and prepare for my return to the ring, which I expect to be toward the end of this year.”

Raring to get back in action, Prajanchai is eager to run it against whoever emerges victorious between Sam-A and Aliff this Friday, and leave no doubt by unifying the throne.

Still, Prajanchai’s sights aren’t limited to one path. He’s also acknowledged unfinished business with undisputed ONE Strawweight Kickboxing World Champion Jonathan Di Bella. The fierce rivals have split their first two meetings, with Prajanchai eager to avenge his most recent setback, a grueling five-round war that cost him two-sport supremacy.

The former dual-belt ONE World Champion said:

“As for my return fight, whether ONE wants me to defend my Muay Thai World Title right away or take a kickboxing match first to get back into the groove, it’s completely up to the schedule ONE gives me. I’m ready for anything. I just want to fight.”

title: What team is LeBron James going to? LeBron James next team odds
description: The Philadelphia 76ers are among a handful of teams making a pitch to sign free the NBA legend. See Lebron James next team odds.
fullText:
Do the Philadelphia 76ers really have a shot?

Following the announcement he wouldn't re-sign with the Los Angeles Lakers, NBA legend LeBron James has made it clear to teams that he would consider signing with a franchise that best fit his needs.

What those are aren't exactly clear, but the Sixers are reportedly one of the teams in the mix for James' services for the upcoming 2026-2027 NBA season — and possibly beyond.

See where the Sixers currently stand among James' suitors.

As of July 23, the Sixers ranked among the top possible teams to sign the all-time NBA leading scorer, according to prediction market Kalshi.

Here's where they rank as of 9 a.m. ET July 23:

Cleveland Cavaliers 27% (down 1%)

Golden State Warriors 17% (up 3%)

Minnesota Timberwolves 2% (down 1%)

Here's the breakdown of James' current odds of signing, according to market prediction site Polymarket:

Cleveland Cavaliers 25% (down 162%)

Golden State Warriors 17% (down 9%)

The Miami Heat accidentally posted a scheduled YouTube livestream titled “LeBronJames Introductory Press Conference” for July 27, a team spokesperson told ESPN's Shams Charania.

The link was mistakenly published while preparing for a possible James signing. It has since been deleted.

While most believe James will return to his hometown Cleveland to close out his career, there's a chance a he turns down a third reunion with the Cavs and signs elsewhere.

Recently, James' agent Rich Paul of Klutch Sports discussed a number of teams his client was considering on his “Game Over” podcast with Max Kellerman.

On a white board, Paul and Kellerman went through the pros of James signing with each team and their starting rosters. The list included:

According to a report by Charania, those teams seemed to have been cut down to the Cavaliers, Heat and 76ers.

Charania said executives for interested teams have been sending voice notes to Paul, who passed them along to James.

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia," Charania said in a July 14 report. " ... This could be any day, any week, now," but that James was getting closer to a decision.

@ShamsCharania latest update on LeBron James' free agency 👑

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia." pic.twitter.com/0P3Cv0F93O

According to Kalshi, here's when James is expected to sign with a new team:

While appearing with 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia last week, ESPN's Dave McMenamin said it was likely that James would make his decision this week.

"A source familiar with LeBron really believes LeBron is nearing the decision ... People around the league are hoping that it comes next week," McMenamin said.

"... I would say there is a pretty decent chance we find out next week. I do believe, strongly, that the Sixers are a legitimate option for him still. I was told, as of last night, he still hasn't made his final decision."

A report the same day by Charania said he spoke with Paul the morning of July 20 and that there's no decision by James and no timetable on a decision. Charania said his understanding is focus remains on Miami, Cleveland, Philadelphia.

However, Paul said on his “Game Over” podcast on July 20 that there is no timetable for James to sign with a new team.

“We’re not going to be rushed,” Paul said.

Speculation that the Heat are now the frontrunners for James' services after Miami jumped ahead of Cleveland in the prediction markets over the weekend.

During a July 16 report, Charania said James' decision was expected soon.

"I think this process is getting closer and closer to the end here," Charania said. "LeBron James has all the information that he needs."

BREAKING: LeBron James is rumoured to be signing with a team VERY soon, per @ShamsCharania.

📈 Shams says that LeBron, after facing criticism for taking too long, has all the information to make a final choice.

There are rumours that LeBron could be doing a "big event" for it. pic.twitter.com/6KK2T8udZN

— BallersCulture (@BallersCultureX) July 17, 2026

Reports were speculating that James would announce his signing during Fanatics Fest, which was held last week in the Javits Center in New York.

But during events on July 16 and 17, James declined to say if had made a free-agent decision yet.

Tyrese Haliburton to LeBron James: 

“What are you going on, what is this… Year 76?”

I see what you did there 😏 pic.twitter.com/RMv5kzYeMw

— RB (@RBPhillyTake) July 16, 2026

"I won't hold you guys up too much longer," James said.

During his Fanatics appearance, James even said the phrase "trust the process," which Sixers fans were quick to connect to the Sixers' longtime rebuilding era with Embiid.

As LeBron James rattles off what he will value in his next team, he used the phrase “trust the process” and the crowd was sent into a tizzy, thinking he was giving a clue about the Philadelphia 76ers. pic.twitter.com/klpBWePWNq

— Dave McMenamin (@mcten) July 17, 2026

Another report over the weekend stated that Sixers owner Josh Harris also spoke to James briefly during Fanatics Fest.

Bob Cooney of On Pattison said on July 20 that he has a strong feeling that James will sign with Philadelphia.

"I am getting a strong feeling he is coming here. I really am," Cooney said. "Why is a 42-year-old man going out there and throwing out 'Trust the Process' and '76 Years,' and all these things that are little hints?

"… Why would you go around teasing stuff like that? … Why are insiders hinting at it now? … Over the last couple of days, I started thinking, you know what, he is putting it out there. I think he might be coming to the Sixers. A couple of weeks ago, you asked me for a percentage, and I said 15-20%. I am upping that now; I think it is 50-50 at least."

During his appearance on 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia, McMenamin said it's top Sixers players Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey and newly acquired forward Jaylen Brown who are currently in contact with James. and not Philly brass such as Bob Meyers, Mike Gansey and Nick Nurse.

"I want to say the people that I've spoken to told me about (Joel) Embiid's interaction, it's been mostly through text messaging, which is an obvious way to communicate in 2026," McMenamin said. "As far as my understanding, it's really been (Tyrese) Maxey as the primary spearhead in terms of keeping that communication and dialogue going. Maxey knows LeBron the best out of those three players

"… I've covered a bunch of teams, and it's not very often where you get all three, if you do have three stars, on the same page moving in the same direction," he said. "It's significant that, from the top down, Bob Myers, Nick Nurse, Mike Gansey, and the three best players on the team are all together on this, saying, 'LeBron, we want you, we need you, and we can do great things if you come here.'"

If James were to sign with Philadelphia, the Sixers would potentially have one of the most talented starting lineups in the NBA. Here's what it might look it:

Sixers potential starting lineup

This article originally appeared on Delaware News Journal: What team is LeBron James going to? LeBron James next team odds

title: El Paso's unbeaten boxing prospect Ivy Enriquez out with knee injury
description: Unbeaten bantamweight prospect Ivy Enriquez is out of her upcoming El Paso fight on Aug. 7 after suffering a torn ACL injury.
fullText:
El Paso's unbeaten bantamweight boxing prospect Ivy Enriquez suffered a torn ACL injury in her right knee during training and will not fight next month at the El Paso County Coliseum.

Enriquez was scheduled to fight Perla “La Princesa” Lomeli on Friday, Aug. 7 in a scheduled eight round fight. The Enriquez vs. Lomeli was the expected co-main event.

Red Owl Boxing is promoting the fight card, with the main event set to stream on DAZN at 8 p.m. MT/9 p.m. CT. The preliminary portion of the card will stream live on Red Owl Boxing’s YouTube channel, beginning at 6 p.m. MT./7 p.m. CT.

Enriquez, who is a 2025 Bel Air High School graduate and won multiple national amateur boxing championships and fought internationally.

In the main event, world-ranked Emanuel Moreno (13-0, 3 KO’s) of Wichita Falls, Texas, will battle it out with Guillermo Gutierrez (13-3, 1 KO) of San Antonio, Texas, for Moreno’s World Boxing Association Continental Americas bantamweight championship at stake.

In a featured women’s super welterweight contest, Ariana Carrasco (1-0, 1 KO) of Las Cruces will take on Chanisse Jenner (0-0) of Ashford, Kent, U.K.

Welterweight Devon Garcia (4-0, 4 KO’s) of Las Cruces and Patrick Harris (1-6, 1 KO) of Killeen, Texas will fight in a four-rounder.

Felix F. Chavez can be reached at fchavez@elpasotimes.com; @Fchavezeptimes on X

This article originally appeared on El Paso Times: El Paso's unbeaten boxing prospect Ivy Enriquez out with knee injury

title: Tzolis completes move to Arsenal from Club Brugge
description: Arsenal have completed the £34 million ($45 million) signing of winger Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge. The Greece international, 24, has signed a five-year contract with the Premier League champions and arrives as a replacement for Leandro Trossard following his switch to Besiktas.
fullText:
Arsenal have completed the £34 million ($45 million) signing of winger Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge.

The Greece international, 24, has signed a five-year contract with the Premier League champions and arrives as a replacement for Leandro Trossard following his switch to Besiktas.

Tzolis’ move to the Emirates Stadium – Arsenal’s third permanent transfer of the close season – came 48 hours after the north Londoners missed out on Morgan Rogers who signed a £117 million British-record fee with Chelsea, describing his new employers as the “biggest club in London.”

Tzolis scored 43 times in 108 appearances for Club Brugge, and lined up in the Belgians’ 3-0 Champions League defeat to Mikel Arteta’s side last season.

Arsenal’s sporting director Andrea Berta said in a statement on Thursday: “We’re delighted to have completed the signing of Christos Tzolis.

“Christos is an extremely versatile attacking player who naturally operates on the left but is comfortable across the front line. He’s an excellent finisher with both feet, thrives in tight spaces, and possesses incredible technical ability.

“Christos has produced outstanding numbers in terms of goals and assists over the last three seasons, and he’s a player who will raise the technical level of our squad, while bringing positive energy, enthusiasm, and a strong mentality to our team.

“We’re confident Christos will make a significant contribution to our club, and we hope our supporters will enjoy watching him play in an Arsenal shirt. We look forward to sharing this exciting new chapter together.”

Tzolis played 14 Premier League games for Norwich during the 2021-22 campaign but did not find the back of the net. Loan periods with FC Twente and Fortuna Dusseldorf followed before he made the move to Club Brugge in 2024.

Tzolis follows Piero Hincapie – who turned his temporary deal with Arsenal into a full-time switch – and goalkeeper Illan Meslier, a free signing from Leeds, as the Gunners’ third signing.

The announcement arrives as a boost of sorts for Arteta after defender William Saliba was this week ruled out for an “extensive period” with a back problem which curtailed his World Cup. The France international is set to miss the start of Arsenal’s Premier League defence.

title: 'Crashed hard' - Karius recalls difficult years before Schalke joy
description: Schalke goalkeeper Loris Karius has spoken openly about the dark thoughts that entered his mind when his career nosedived, but says he is now feeling positive as he looks to make his Bundesliga comeback. Schalke were promoted to the top flight in May after Karius had a strong season in the second tier.
fullText:
Schalke goalkeeper Loris Karius has spoken openly about the dark thoughts that entered his mind when his career nosedived, but says he is now feeling positive as he looks to make his Bundesliga comeback.

Schalke were promoted to the top flight in May after Karius had a strong season in the second tier. It contrasted markedly with his 2018 Champions League final howlers against Real Madrid, which caused him to leave runner-ups Liverpool and go Besiktas, where he was never happy.

"I sometimes tortured myself to go to work and only tried to get through the day in a reasonably normal way," Karius told the Funke Media Group of the period 2018-2020.

"Even today I do not know what I should have done differently. I simply felt empty, I even thought about ending my career, but I was only 25 years old and could not just stay at home," Karius added. "In that phase I felt nobody would understand me."

The now 33-year-old has rarely talked about the two mistakes which led to goals in Madrid's 3-1 win in 2018. Liverpool gained closure by winning the trophy the next season, but Karius was then in Istanbul.

"Up to that point my career had only been going steeply upwards, then I suddenly crashed hard - all the way down to the bottom," he said.

"It was extremely tough, also because in my mid-20s at the time I was not yet as stable as a person as I am today. And honestly, in that phase I lost the joy of football. I did not feel like training any more, I did not feel like playing any more. Those were difficult years."

He confided in a mental health coach at the time but that did not help. "I still could not flip the switch. Over the years I somehow managed to fight my way out of this low on my own," Karius added.

After reserve stints at Union Berlin and Newcastle, he was initially without a club until he found a new sporting home at Schalke in January 2025.

"Who else can say they play in front of 60,000 fans every other week? Otherwise only absolute superstars from the music business can do that - you have to keep reminding yourself of it," Karius said about Schalke.

title: Hot takes: Who is going to be last in the East?
description: A friend of mine offered an interesting question, mostly just to chat about for a little and move on but it’s been sticking in my mind for a while now. The question was who will be in last place in the Eastern Conference next season? It’s been an interesting point to ponder on in the […]
fullText:
A friend of mine offered an interesting question, mostly just to chat about for a little and move on but it’s been sticking in my mind for a while now. The question was who will be in last place in the Eastern Conference next season?

It’s been an interesting point to ponder on in the dead of summer. Not all the teams are particularly good, but no one is currently trying to be bad by punting the season through a long-term rebuilding strategy these days that has made themselves a poor team either. There are no Chicago’s or Calgary’s in the East these days that will drop far behind the pack and await what they hope will be a brighter future. All 16 teams in the East, to varying degrees, have the goal, desire and something of an expectation to make the playoffs next season.

That shapes up to mean chaos and fun. Take a look at last season – the three worst teams in the Eastern Conference ended up being Florida (the 2x defending Stanley Cup champions), Toronto (the team that finished second in points for the conference the year prior in 2024-25) and the New York Rangers (who were first in the East as recently as 2023-24).

That’s probably three teams that this time 12 months ago almost no one would have guessed would be at the bottom of the conference, although there were some warning signs with NYR and Aleksander Barkov’s preseason injury did impact the Panthers in a predictable way before the year started. If anything in the modern NHL, you might as well throw out the results of the prior year when attempting to forecast the future. The base of what has happened isn’t going to be a roadmap for what is to come, by and large for many teams.

Going off my preseason predictions, I would have had Philadelphia, Boston and Pittsburgh as the bottom-three teams in 2025-26, all of which you might notice made the playoffs. That’s pretty humbling to undertake the projection of who could be the worst in 2026-27.

So who is going to end up 16th out of 16 teams? Let’s see how it could happen with some candidates, in no specific order.

Detroit Red Wings – There are a lot of pieces of strife here: the captain wants a trade, the franchise legend GM got kicked upstairs, one of their best players in Alex Debrincat is an impending free agent with an uncertain future, their defense is mostly a collection of trash besides one great player and their goaltending is borderline shaky. Maybe the Red Wings pull the nose up on this thing, but isn’t a challenge to imagine that things get worse before they get better.

New York Rangers – We liked their offseason in terms of immediate upgrades to the team, but last year’s last place team still has a lot consternation hanging over it like a black fog. This team looks like they’re one Igor Shesterkin injury away from disaster. Of course, that could be said of many if their best player was out, but I think the Rangers this year have a low ceiling and an even lower floor that puts them in the conversation of being a low-end team.

Pittsburgh Penguins – The top five returning scorers range in age of 33-40, eventually the core that was strong enough to power the Pens to the playoffs last season is going to lose strength. Several supporting players all had career seasons last year and could be difficult to duplicate. The top-three goaltenders in the organization have combined for 78 career NHL games entering the season, the defensive numbers were among the worst in the league without much in the way of upgrades.

New York Islanders – The Islanders strategy seems to be ‘let’s hope Peter DeBoer can make some magic’, because they’ve done almost nothing this off-season to improve. They’ve lost multiple captain-type of players recently (first Brock Nelson in 2025, then Anders Lee this summer). Matthew Schaefer and Ilya Sorokin (plus Bo Horvat and Mat Barzal) might given them enough talent to escape last place, but this also looks like a situation where a new GM needs some burdensome contracts to tick away while replenishing the system with young talent, a process that sometimes means enduring pain along the way.

Columbus Blue Jackets – Their top six players from last season: Zach Werenski (might not sign with them again, was almost traded before situation smoothed out), Kirill Marchenko (reportedly doesn’t want to re-sign), Adam Fantilli (RFA, still unsigned), Charlie Coyle (re-signed as an overpayment), Boone Jenner and Mason Marchment (both left as free agents). Looks very unsettled right now for an organization known to take one step forward followed by shuffling towards the back.

Buffalo Sabres – They lost their second-highest scoring forward (Alex Tuch) and second-highest scoring defenseman (Bowen Byram) with no replacement, save Olen Zellweger. The highest paid forward (Josh Norris) has missed more time with injury than he’s played in the last four seasons. Their current goalies (Alex Lyon and Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen) don’t inspire a lot of confidence, though rumors of a Connor Hellebuyck trade would surely elevate Buffalo into a strong position – if it materializes. It would be shocking if the Sabres ended up all the way at the bottom, and they showed they have a very high ceiling if their young core takes another step, but I don’t know, can’t shake the feeling that they have elements of coming back to the pack after a tremendous season.

We just blurbed on six teams, not far off from half the conference. The options added up quickly, even though the cases for all could be stretches and dependent on worst case scenarios happening. Maybe one of these teams finish last, but then again it wouldn’t be a shocker if several of them end up making the playoffs next season either. That’s the NHL these days, filled with parity and unexpected outcomes. About all we can know for sure is that some team out there is going to finish at the bottom, and much like last season’s lower-end teams, they probably will be wondering just how it ended up that way.

title: Thursday Dots: Zrno Excuses
description: The only two things in life that make it worth livin’ Are guitars that tune good and firm feelin’ Dots
fullText:
The only two things in life that make it worth livin’

Are guitars that tune good and firm feelin’ Dots

Not content to have Nikola Dzepina as the only player whose last name starts with a Z adjacent to a consonant, Danny Sprinkle added Rutgers transfer Harun Zrno to the roster on Wednesday. Zrno is a 6’7” wing who carved out a regular rotation spot for Rutgers last season, though his shooting percentages were sub-par and he didn’t pile up impressive numbers of rebounds or assists. Sprinkle and his staff will need to further develop their new addition to make him more than just a body to fill out the roster.

The Dawgs also announced the addition of one of the coaches who will work with Zrno and company next season. The Huskies hired long-time NBA assistant Charles Klask to his staff. Klask most recently worked as an assistant for the Denver Nuggets, and has previously assisted on the staffs of Rick Carlisle, Doc Rivers, Stan Van Gundy, and others.

Dawgman continued their series of position previews as we count down the days to the start of Fall camp with a profile of the WR room. The biggest question to answer in this group is who will step up to fill the sizeable hole left by Denzel Boston’s departure. There might not be a one-for-one possession receiver to slide in for Boston, but Rashid Williams and several newcomers offer lots of upside.

The Softball team saw the Men’s Basketball team adding a Scarlet Knight and raised them with a Scarlette Chapman. The Huskies will bring in the lefty-hitting infielder from Edmonds College, where she absolutely mashed against lesser competition.

Zrno was 107th out of 107 in the B1G in EvanMiya's BPR metric for those that used 1000+ possessions. Shot 34% from deep on 150+ attempts at 6'7 but advanced stats show he didn't add much else. Fine with it as last roster spots but not expecting much. https://t.co/Llf6ez2NPH
